exercises.overview: The single arm cable lateral raise allows for unilateral shoulder development while providing constant tension throughout the range of motion. This exercise is excellent for correcting muscle imbalances and building lateral deltoid strength.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe for most individuals. Those with shoulder impingement should avoid raising the arm above shoulder height and may need to modify the range of motion.

commonMistakes: Rounding the back or hunching shoulders during the movement
Using momentum by swaying back and forth
Pulling the handle too high toward the chest instead of lower ribs
exerciseTips: Keep your torso upright and avoid leaning back during the pull
Focus on pulling with your back muscles rather than your arms
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of each rep
Maintain a controlled tempo on both the pulling and releasing phases
instructions: Sit at a cable row station with your feet firmly planted on the footrests and knees slightly bent.
Grab the handle with both hands and sit up tall with your shoulders back and chest out.
Start with your arms extended and feel a stretch in your lats and middle back.
Pull the handle toward your lower ribs by driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Hold the contracted position briefly, focusing on squeezing your back muscles.
Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position while maintaining good posture.
exercises.name: Cable Low Row
exercises.overview: The cable low row is a beginner-friendly compound pulling exercise that targets the latissimus dorsi and middle back muscles. This seated exercise provides constant tension and allows for controlled movement while building back strength and improving posture.
exercises.safetyInfo: Those with lower back issues should ensure proper seated posture and avoid excessive forward lean. Start with lighter weights to master the movement pattern before progressing to heavier loads.

commonMistakes: Using arms to lift the weight instead of rotating through the core
Standing too upright and not engaging the legs properly
Rotating too quickly without maintaining control
exerciseTips: Keep your core braced throughout the entire movement pattern
Allow your hips and feet to rotate naturally with the movement
Focus on controlled rotation rather than speed
Maintain tension in your core during the return phase
instructions: Set cable at lowest position and attach D-handle or rope attachment.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, side facing the cable machine.
Squat down slightly and grasp handle with both hands near your outside hip.
Rotate your torso diagonally upward across your body toward the opposite shoulder.
Keep your arms relatively straight and let your torso do the work.
Return to starting position with control and complete all reps before switching sides.
exercises.name: Cable Low To High Wood Chop
exercises.name_pt: Wood Chop na Polia de Baixo para Cima
exercises.overview: The cable low to high wood chop trains rotational power from a low position upward, mimicking many athletic and daily movement patterns. This exercise develops core stability and rotational strength in the transverse plane.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with lighter resistance to learn proper movement mechanics. Avoid if you have recent abdominal or lower back injuries until cleared by a healthcare provider.
variations: Cable wood chop high to low

commonMistakes: Allowing elbows to flare outward during movement
Using momentum to swing the weight up
Lowering the bar too far past the forehead
exerciseTips: Keep upper arms perpendicular to torso throughout movement
Control the negative portion slowly to maximize muscle tension
Stop just before bar touches forehead to maintain constant tension
Squeeze triceps hard at the top position
instructions: Lie on a flat bench and grasp the straight bar attachment of a low pulley with a narrow overhand grip. Tip: The easiest way to do this is to have someone hand you the bar as you lay down.
With your arms extended, position the bar over your torso. Your arms and your torso should create a 90-degree angle. This will be your starting position.

Lower the bar by bending at the elbow while keeping the upper arms stationary and elbows in. Go down until the bar lightly touches your forehead. Breathe in as you perform this portion of the movement.
Flex the triceps as you lift the bar back to its starting position. Exhale as you perform this portion of the movement.
Hold for a second at the contracted position and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Cable Lying Triceps Extension
exercises.name_pt: Extensão de Tríceps Deitado na Polia
exercises.overview: The cable lying triceps extension targets all three heads of the triceps while providing constant tension throughout the range of motion. This exercise is excellent for building tricep strength and size with reduced joint stress compared to free weights.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have elbow tendonitis or shoulder impingement. Use controlled movements to prevent the cable from snapping back unexpectedly.

commonMistakes: Moving the upper arm away from the torso
Using too much weight and compensating with body movement
Not achieving full elbow extension at the bottom
exerciseTips: Keep upper arm locked against your side throughout the movement
Use a full range of motion from bent to fully extended
Focus on squeezing the tricep at the bottom position
Maintain stable stance with opposite leg forward
instructions: With your right hand, grasp a single handle attached to the high-cable pulley using a supinated (underhand; palms facing up) grip. You should be standing directly in front of the weight stack.

Now pull the handle down so that your upper arm and elbow are locked in to the side of your body. Your upper arm and forearm should form an acute angle (less than 90-degrees). You can keep the other arm by the waist and you can have one leg in front of you and the other one back for better balance. This will be your starting position.

As you contract the triceps, move the single handle attachment down to your side until your arm is straight. Breathe out as you perform this movement. Tip: Only the forearms should move. Your upper arms should remain stationary at all times.
Squeeze the triceps and hold for a second in this contracted position.
Slowly return the handle to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and then perform the same movement with the other arm.
exercises.name: Cable One Arm Tricep Extension
exercises.name_pt: Extensão de Tríceps Unilateral na Polia
exercises.overview: The cable one arm tricep extension allows for unilateral training to address strength imbalances while providing constant tension. This isolation exercise effectively targets all three tricep heads with enhanced mind-muscle connection.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with lighter weight to establish proper form. Avoid this exercise during acute elbow pain or inflammation.

commonMistakes: Allowing elbows to flare out to the sides during the movement
Using shoulder movement instead of isolating the triceps
Moving the upper arms instead of keeping them stationary
exerciseTips: Keep your elbows stationary and close to your head throughout the movement
Focus on moving only your forearms while keeping your upper arms fixed
Maintain a slight forward lean to optimize the angle of pull
Control both the extending and returning phases of the movement
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the highest position and attach a rope or straight bar handle.
Grab the handle with both hands and step forward, facing away from the machine.
Lean slightly forward and position your arms overhead with elbows bent at about 90 degrees.
Keep your elbows stationary and close to your head while extending your forearms forward.
Contract your triceps to fully extend your arms, feeling the stretch and contraction.
Slowly return to the starting position with your elbows bent and repeat the movement.
exercises.name: Cable Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.name_pt: Extensão de Tríceps na Polia Acima da Cabeça
exercises.overview: The cable overhead tricep extension is a beginner-friendly isolation exercise that targets all three heads of the triceps with emphasis on the long head. The overhead position provides an excellent stretch and allows for constant tension throughout the range of motion.
exercises.safetyInfo: Those with shoulder impingement or elbow tendinitis should use lighter weights and avoid excessive shoulder flexion. Stop if you experience sharp pain in the elbows or shoulders.

commonMistakes: Allowing shoulders to round forward during the movement
Using momentum to swing the weight up
Letting elbows hyperextend at the bottom position
exerciseTips: Keep chest pressed firmly against the preacher pad
Control the eccentric phase slowly for maximum muscle damage
Avoid fully locking out elbows at the bottom to maintain tension
Squeeze biceps hard at the peak contraction
instructions: Place a preacher bench about 2 feet in front of a pulley machine.
Attach a straight bar to the low pulley.
Sit at the preacher bench with your elbow and upper arms firmly on top of the bench pad and have someone hand you the bar from the low pulley.
Grab the bar and fully extend your arms on top of the preacher bench pad. This will be your starting position.
Now start pilling the weight up towards your shoulders and squeeze the biceps hard at the top of the movement. Exhale as you perform this motion. Also, hold for a second at the top.
Now slowly lower the weight to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Cable Preacher Curl
exercises.overview: The cable preacher curl provides constant tension while isolating the biceps in a fixed position. This exercise is excellent for building bicep peak and improving the mind-muscle connection.
exercises.safetyInfo: Stop short of full elbow extension to prevent hyperextension injury. Avoid heavy weights that force you to use momentum, which can strain the bicep tendon.
variations: Seated One-Arm Dumbbell Palms-Down Wrist Curl

commonMistakes: Lifting the shoulder or leaning away from the pad during the curl
Using momentum or swinging to complete the repetition
Allowing the elbow to lift off the preacher pad during the movement
exerciseTips: Keep your wrist straight and strong throughout the movement
Focus on squeezing your biceps at the top of each rep
Maintain constant tension by not fully locking out your elbow
Control the negative portion to maximize muscle activation
instructions: Set up a preacher bench in front of a low cable machine and attach a single handle.
Sit on the preacher bench with your working arm extended over the pad, grasping the cable handle.
Position your armpit at the top edge of the preacher pad with your arm fully extended.
Curl the handle up by contracting your biceps until your forearm is vertical.
Slowly lower the handle back to the starting position with control and repeat for all reps before switching arms.
exercises.name: Cable Preacher Curl Single Arm
exercises.overview: The Cable Preacher Curl Single Arm isolates the biceps while providing constant tension throughout the range of motion. Training one arm at a time helps identify and correct strength imbalances between limbs.
exercises.safetyInfo: The preacher position can place stress on the elbow joint, especially at the bottom of the movement. Warm up thoroughly and avoid bouncing at the bottom position to prevent biceps or elbow injuries.

commonMistakes: Using too much weight and involving the lats
Allowing arms to drift above or below shoulder height
Rushing through the movement without controlling the tempo
exerciseTips: Initiate movement by squeezing shoulder blades together
Keep arms parallel to the floor throughout the movement
Focus on pulling with the rear delts, not the arms
Pause briefly at the peak contraction
instructions: Adjust the pulleys to the appropriate height and adjust the weight. The pulleys should be above your head.
Grab the left pulley with your right hand and the right pulley with your left hand, crossing them in front of you. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the movement by moving your arms back and outward, keeping your arms straight as you execute the movement.
Pause at the end of the motion before returning the handles to the start position.
exercises.name: Cable Rear Delt Fly
exercises.overview: The cable rear delt fly specifically targets the often-neglected posterior deltoids while improving shoulder stability and posture. This exercise is crucial for balanced shoulder development and injury prevention.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with light weight to master the movement pattern. Avoid if you have recent shoulder surgery or severe shoulder impingement.
variations: Bent Over Two-Dumbbell Row With Palms In

commonMistakes: Using hip flexors instead of abs to lift the legs
Allowing lower back to arch excessively
Moving too quickly through the range of motion
exerciseTips: Focus on lifting hips up rather than just bringing knees in
Exhale forcefully as you contract your abs
Control the return to starting position slowly
Keep lower back pressed to the floor
instructions: Connect an ankle strap attachment to a low pulley cable and position a mat on the floor in front of it.
Sit down with your feet toward the pulley and attach the cable to your ankles.
Lie down, elevate your legs and bend your knees at a 90-degree angle. Your legs and the cable should be aligned. If not, adjust the pulley up or down until they are.
With your hands behind your head, bring your knees inward to your torso and elevate your hips off the floor.
Pause for a moment and in a slow and controlled manner drop your hips and bring your legs back to the starting 90-degree angle. You should still have tension on your abs in the resting position.
Repeat the same movement to failure.
exercises.name: Cable Reverse Crunch
exercises.overview: The cable reverse crunch targets the lower portion of the rectus abdominis while providing variable resistance. This exercise complements traditional crunches for complete abdominal development.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have lower back issues or hip flexor tightness. Start with bodyweight reverse crunches before adding cable resistance.

commonMistakes: Using too much weight and compensating with shoulder movement
Allowing wrists to flex or bend during the curl
Moving elbows away from the body during the lift
exerciseTips: Keep your wrists straight and neutral throughout the movement
Focus on squeezing your forearms at the top of the curl
Maintain slow and controlled tempo, especially on the lowering phase
Avoid swinging or using momentum to lift the weight
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the lowest position and attach a straight bar handle.
Stand facing the cable machine with feet shoulder-width apart, grasping the bar with an overhand grip, hands about shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your sides and shoulders pulled back with a slight bend in your knees.
Curl the bar up by flexing your forearms while keeping your wrists straight and elbows stationary.
Squeeze at the top position for a brief pause, then slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
Maintain control throughout the entire range of motion and repeat for desired repetitions.
exercises.name: Cable Reverse Curl
exercises.overview: The cable reverse curl targets the forearm extensors and brachialis muscles using an overhand grip. This exercise helps build forearm strength and improves grip strength while balancing the development between flexors and extensors.
exercises.safetyInfo: Individuals with wrist or elbow injuries should use lighter weights and focus on proper form. Stop immediately if you experience sharp pain in the forearms or wrists.

commonMistakes: Using too much arm instead of focusing on rear delts
Allowing the shoulders to roll forward during the movement
Using momentum instead of controlled movement
exerciseTips: Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together
Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout
Lead the movement with your pinky fingers
Control the weight on both the pulling and return phases
instructions: Set the cable pulleys to chest height and attach handles to both sides.
Stand in the center and grab the left handle with your right hand and right handle with your left hand.
Step back to create tension with your arms extended and crossed in front of your chest.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and pull the handles apart in a wide arc.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement.
Slowly return to the starting position with control, maintaining tension.
exercises.name: Cable Reverse Fly
exercises.overview: The cable reverse fly targets the often-neglected rear deltoids and upper back muscles. It helps improve posture and balance the shoulder muscles for overall shoulder health.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe for most people. Those with shoulder impingement should start with light weight and focus on proper shoulder blade movement.

commonMistakes: Allowing elbows to flare out to the sides
Using too much shoulder movement instead of isolated tricep work
Not achieving full stretch at the bottom of the movement
exerciseTips: Keep elbows close to your head throughout the movement
Step forward to create proper cable angle and tension
Focus on stretching triceps at the bottom position
Drive elbows up and forward to maximize tricep activation
instructions: Attach a rope to the bottom pulley of the pulley machine.

Grasping the rope with both hands, extend your arms with your hands directly above your head using a neutral grip (palms facing each other). Your elbows should be in close to your head and the arms should be perpendicular to the floor with the knuckles aimed at the ceiling. This will be your starting position.
Slowly lower the rope behind your head as you hold the upper arms stationary. Inhale as you perform this movement and pause when your triceps are fully stretched.
Return to the starting position by flexing your triceps as you breathe out.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Cable Rope Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.name_pt: Extensão de Tríceps na Polia com Corda Acima da Cabeça
exercises.overview: The cable rope overhead triceps extension emphasizes the long head of the triceps through a full stretch position. This exercise is excellent for building overall tricep mass and improving overhead pressing strength.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure proper shoulder mobility before performing this exercise. Avoid if you have shoulder impingement or recent tricep/elbow injuries.

commonMistakes: Allowing elbows to drop below shoulder level
Using too much weight and compromising form
Pulling too low toward the chest instead of upper chest
exerciseTips: Keep elbows elevated at shoulder height throughout the movement
Pull the rope to just below neck level
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top
Control the negative portion of each rep
instructions: Sit in the same position on a low pulley row station as you would if you were doing seated cable rows for the back.
Attach a rope to the pulley and grasp it with an overhand grip. Your arms should be extended and parallel to the floor with the elbows flared out.
Keep your lower back upright and slide your hips back so that your knees are slightly bent. This will be your starting position.

Pull the cable attachment towards your upper chest, just below the neck, as you keep your elbows up and out to the sides. Continue this motion as you exhale until the elbows travel slightly behind the back. Tip: Keep your upper arms horizontal, perpendicular to the torso and parallel to the floor throughout the motion.
Go back to the initial position where the arms are extended and the shoulders are stretched forward. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Cable Rope Rear-Delt Rows
exercises.name_pt: Remada para Deltoide Posterior na Polia com Corda
exercises.overview: This exercise targets the often-neglected posterior deltoids while improving shoulder stability and posture. It's essential for balancing pressing movements and preventing shoulder dysfunction.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid using excessive weight that causes the shoulders to round forward or compromises posture. Those with shoulder impingement should start with lighter resistance.

commonMistakes: Allowing hips to sag during the exercise
Using momentum instead of controlled movement
Rotating too quickly without proper control
exerciseTips: Maintain elevated hips throughout the movement
Keep arms fully extended during rotation
Control the tempo on both directions
Engage core before initiating rotation
instructions: Connect a standard handle attachment, and position the cable to a middle pulley position.
Lie on a stability ball perpendicular to the cable and grab the handle with one hand. You should be approximately arm's length away from the pulley, with the tension of the weight on the cable.
Grab the handle with both hands and fully extend your arms above your chest. You hands should be directly in-line with the pulley. If not, adjust the pulley up or down until they are.
Keep your hips elevated and abs engaged. Rotate your torso away from the pulley for a full-quarter rotation. Your body should be flat from head to knees.
Pause for a moment and in a slow and controlled manner reset to the starting position. You should still have side tension on the cable in the resting position.
Repeat the same movement to failure.
Then, reposition and repeat the same series of movements on the opposite side.
exercises.name: Cable Russian Twists
exercises.overview: This dynamic core exercise combines stability and rotation training while challenging the entire abdominal complex. The cable provides constant tension throughout the rotational movement.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure proper stability ball positioning to prevent falls. Those with lower back issues should avoid excessive rotation and use lighter resistance.

commonMistakes: Using arms and shoulders instead of abs
Allowing the weight to hyperextend the spine excessively
Moving too quickly without muscle control
exerciseTips: Focus on flexing the spine rather than pulling with arms
Keep elbows close to your body throughout
Control the eccentric portion slowly
Breathe out forcefully at the bottom of each rep
instructions: Seat on a flat bench with your back facing a high pulley.

Grasp the cable rope attachment with both hands (with the palms of the hands facing each other) and place your hands securely over both shoulders. Tip: Allow the weight to hyperextend the lower back slightly. This will be your starting position.
With the hips stationary, flex the waist so the elbows travel toward the hips. Breathe out as you perform this step.
As you inhale, go back to the initial position slowly.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Cable Seated Crunch
exercises.overview: This seated variation provides constant tension on the abdominals while offering better stability than standing versions. The cable allows for progressive overload of the core muscles.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with light weight to master the movement pattern. Excessive hyperextension can strain the lower back, so control the starting position carefully.

commonMistakes: Lifting arms above shoulder height
Using too much weight and compromising form
Allowing torso to assist the movement
exerciseTips: Maintain slight bend in elbows throughout movement
Lift arms to parallel with floor, no higher
Keep torso stable against thighs
Control the lowering phase
instructions: Stand in the middle of two low pulleys that are opposite to each other and place a flat bench right behind you (in perpendicular fashion to you; the narrow edge of the bench should be the one behind you). Select the weight to be used on each pulley.
Now sit at the edge of the flat bench behind you with your feet placed in front of your knees.
Bend forward while keeping your back flat and rest your torso on the thighs.

Have someone give you the single handles attached to the pulleys. Grasp the left pulley with the right hand and the right pulley with the left after you select your weight. The pulleys should run under your knees and your arms will be extended with palms facing each other and a slight bend at the elbows. This will be the starting position.

While keeping the arms stationary, raise the upper arms to the sides until they are parallel to the floor and at shoulder height. Exhale during the execution of this movement and hold the contraction for a second.
Slowly lower your arms to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions. Tip: Maintain upper arms perpendicular to torso and a fixed elbow position (10 degree to 30 degree angle) throughout exercise.
exercises.name: Cable Seated Lateral Raise
exercises.overview: This seated variation eliminates cheating and momentum while providing constant tension throughout the range of motion. It effectively isolates the lateral deltoids for shoulder width development.
exercises.safetyInfo: The seated position reduces momentum but may increase stress on the shoulder joint. Those with shoulder impingement should use lighter weight and avoid lifting above parallel.

commonMistakes: Using too much momentum and rocking the torso back and forth
Pulling the handle too high toward the neck instead of the lower chest
Allowing the shoulders to roll forward at the starting position
exerciseTips: Keep your torso upright throughout the movement and avoid leaning back excessively
Focus on initiating the pull with your back muscles rather than your arms
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of each rep
Control the weight on both the pulling and returning phases
instructions: Sit at the cable row station with your feet on the footrests and knees slightly bent.
Grasp the handle with both hands and sit up tall with your shoulders back and chest up.
Pull the handle toward your lower chest by driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Slowly extend your arms back to the starting position while maintaining an upright posture.
exercises.name: Cable Seated Row
exercises.overview: The cable seated row is a horizontal pulling exercise that strengthens the latissimus dorsi and middle back muscles. It's an essential movement for developing a strong, balanced back and improving posture.
exercises.safetyInfo: Those with lower back problems should ensure proper seated posture and avoid excessive leaning. Start with moderate weight to master the movement pattern.

commonMistakes: Arching the back excessively during the press
Pressing the weight forward instead of straight up
Not maintaining equal tension in both cables
exerciseTips: Keep core engaged throughout the movement
Press handles directly overhead, not forward
Maintain neutral spine position
Control the descent to shoulder level
instructions: Move the cables to the bottom of the towers and select an appropriate weight.
Stand directly in between the uprights. Grasp the cables and hold them at shoulder height, palms facing forward. This will be your starting position.
Keeping your head and chest up, extend through the elbow to press the handles directly over head.
After pausing at the top, return to the starting position and repeat.
exercises.name: Cable Shoulder Press
exercises.overview: This bilateral pressing movement provides constant tension while challenging shoulder stability and core strength. The cable system offers a unique resistance curve compared to free weights.
exercises.safetyInfo: Those with shoulder impingement or lower back issues should use lighter weight and ensure proper core engagement. Avoid pressing behind the head.

commonMistakes: Rolling shoulders in a circular motion
Using biceps to assist the movement
Not achieving full range of motion at the top
exerciseTips: Elevate shoulders straight up, not forward or back
Hold the peak contraction for one second
Keep arms extended throughout the movement
Focus on squeezing the upper traps
instructions: Grasp a cable bar attachment that is attached to a low pulley with a shoulder width or slightly wider overhand (palms facing down) grip.
Stand erect close to the pulley with your arms extended in front of you holding the bar. This will be your starting position.

Lift the bar by elevating the shoulders as high as possible as you exhale. Hold the contraction at the top for a second. Tip: The arms should remain extended at all times. Refrain from using the biceps to help lift the bar. Only the shoulders should be moving up and down.
Lower the bar back to the original position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Cable Shrugs
exercises.overview: This exercise specifically targets the upper trapezius muscles for improved neck and shoulder stability. The cable provides consistent tension throughout the shrugging motion.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid excessive weight that causes the shoulders to round forward. Those with neck issues should perform the movement slowly and avoid extreme ranges.

commonMistakes: Moving the elbow forward during the curl
Using the shoulder to help lift the weight
Releasing tension too quickly on the lowering phase
exerciseTips: Keep your elbow stationary throughout the movement
Squeeze your bicep hard at the top of each rep
Control the weight on the way down for maximum muscle tension
Avoid swinging or using momentum
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the lowest position and attach a single handle.
Stand facing the cable machine with feet shoulder-width apart.
Grab the handle with one hand using an underhand grip, arm fully extended.
Keep your elbow close to your side and curl the handle toward your shoulder.
Squeeze your bicep at the top of the movement, then slowly lower with control.
Complete all reps on one arm before switching to the other side.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Bicep Curl
exercises.overview: The cable single arm bicep curl isolates the biceps muscle while providing constant tension throughout the range of motion. The unilateral nature helps identify and correct strength imbalances.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe for most people. Those with elbow tendinitis should use lighter weight and focus on controlled movements.

commonMistakes: Placing too much weight on the rear foot
Using only the arms to pull the cable
Allowing the front knee to drift inward
exerciseTips: Keep most of your weight on your front leg
Pull the cable as you drive up from the bottom position
Maintain an upright torso throughout the movement
Control both the descent and the cable resistance
instructions: Set a cable at chest height and stand with your back to the machine holding the handle.
Place your rear foot on a bench behind you in the Bulgarian split squat position.
Create tension in the cable by taking a step forward with your front leg.
Lower into a lunge while pulling the cable handle toward your chest.
Drive through your front heel while continuing the cable pull to return to start.
Complete all reps on one side before switching legs and arm.
keywords: cable rear foot elevated split squat
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.overview: This advanced variation combines the Bulgarian split squat with cable resistance to target the legs while engaging the posterior chain. It challenges stability and coordination while providing constant tension.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure the bench is stable and positioned at appropriate distance. Start with lighter cable resistance to master the coordination.

commonMistakes: Using too much arm instead of chest muscle
Allowing the torso to rotate excessively
Moving too fast without feeling the muscle stretch
exerciseTips: Maintain a slight bend in your elbow throughout
Focus on squeezing your chest at the top
Keep your core engaged for stability
Control the eccentric portion slowly
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the lowest position and attach a single handle.
Stand with your side to the cable machine, feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handle with the arm closest to the machine, starting with arm down and back.
With a slight bend in your elbow, sweep your arm up and across your body.
Bring your hand to about chest level or slightly higher.
Slowly return to the starting position and repeat for desired reps.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Chest Fly Low To High
exercises.name_pt: Crucifixo Unilateral na Polia de Baixo para Cima
exercises.overview: The cable single arm chest fly from low to high targets the upper chest fibers while providing constant tension throughout the movement. It's excellent for developing the often-neglected upper portion of the pectoral muscles.
exercises.safetyInfo: Those with shoulder impingement should avoid this exercise or use very light weight. Stop if you feel any pinching sensation in the shoulder joint.

commonMistakes: Standing too close to the cable machine
Allowing excessive torso rotation
Not maintaining tension at the starting position
exerciseTips: Maintain a strong core to resist the rotational force
Keep your shoulders square and avoid twisting
Focus on squeezing your chest at the end of each rep
Step far enough forward to maintain tension throughout the movement
instructions: Set the cable handle to chest height and grab it with one hand.
Step forward into a staggered stance with your opposite foot forward.
Position your arm with a slight bend in your elbow and your hand at chest level.
Press the handle forward and across your body, extending your arm fully.
Return to the starting position with control and repeat for all reps before switching sides.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Chest Press
exercises.overview: The cable single arm chest press is a functional pressing movement that targets the chest while challenging core stability. The cable provides constant tension throughout the range of motion, making it excellent for muscle activation.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe for most individuals but those with shoulder injuries should start with light resistance. Maintain proper posture to avoid lower back strain.
variations: Half Kneeling Single Arm Cable Press

commonMistakes: Allowing the torso to rotate excessively during the pull
Pulling with the arm instead of initiating with the back
Using momentum instead of controlled muscle contraction
exerciseTips: Keep your core engaged to prevent rotation during the pull
Focus on pulling with your back muscles, not your arm
Squeeze your shoulder blade at the end of each repetition
Maintain a slight bend in your knees throughout the movement
instructions: Set cable at chest height and attach a single handle.
Stand arm's length away with feet shoulder-width apart facing the machine.
Grasp the handle with one hand and step back to create tension.
Pull your elbow back while squeezing your shoulder blade toward your spine.
Bring your hand toward your ribs while keeping your elbow close to your body.
Return to starting position with control and complete all reps before switching arms.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm High Row
exercises.overview: The cable single arm high row targets the lats and middle back unilaterally, helping to correct imbalances and improve pulling strength. The single arm variation increases core stability demands and allows for focused muscle development.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe for most individuals. Those with shoulder impingement should adjust the cable height to avoid pain during the rowing motion.

commonMistakes: Raising the arm too high above shoulder level
Using the torso to swing the weight up
Not maintaining tension at the bottom of the movement
exerciseTips: Lead with your pinky finger to maximize shoulder activation
Keep your wrist straight and strong throughout
Control the negative portion of the movement
Avoid using momentum or swinging the weight
instructions: Set the cable handle to the lowest position and grab it with the hand furthest from the machine.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your working arm across your body.
Keep a slight bend in your elbow and your core engaged.
Raise your arm out to the side until it reaches shoulder height.
Lower the weight with control back to the starting position.
Complete all repetitions before switching to the other arm.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Lateral Raise
exercises.name_pt: Elevação Lateral com Um Braço na Polia
exercises.overview: The cable single arm lateral raise is an isolation exercise targeting the lateral deltoid for broader shoulders. The cable provides constant tension throughout the movement, making it superior to dumbbells for muscle activation.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe but those with shoulder impingement should avoid raising the arm above shoulder height. Start with light resistance to assess comfort level.

commonMistakes: Using only the arms to pull the cable instead of rotating the torso
Allowing the back leg to bear too much weight
Rushing through the movement without controlling the rotation
exerciseTips: Initiate the rotation from your core, not your arms
Keep the cable handle close to your body throughout
Maintain balance by keeping most weight on your front leg
Control both the lunge and rotation simultaneously
instructions: Set cable at chest height and grasp handle with the arm farthest from the machine.
Step into a lunge position with the outside leg forward, creating tension in the cable.
Lower into the lunge while simultaneously rotating your torso toward the front leg.
Pause briefly at the bottom with rotation complete.
Return to starting position by pushing up through the front heel while rotating back.
Complete all reps on one side before switching.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Lunge With Rotation
exercises.name_pt: Afundo com Rotação Unilateral na Polia
exercises.overview: This exercise combines a lunge with rotational movement, targeting the legs while engaging the core and improving functional movement patterns. It's excellent for athletes and those wanting to improve rotational power.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have lower back issues or knee injuries. Start with lighter resistance to master the coordination before progressing.

commonMistakes: Using too much lat involvement instead of rear delt
Allowing the shoulder to roll forward
Moving the arm too high or too low
exerciseTips: Focus on pulling with your rear delt, not your lat
Keep your shoulders down and back throughout
Maintain a slight bend in your elbow
Control both the positive and negative portions of the movement
instructions: Set the cable handle to chest height and grab it with the arm opposite to the cable machine.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and extend your working arm across your body.
Keep a slight bend in your elbow and maintain good posture.
Pull the handle across your body and back until your arm is parallel to the floor.
Squeeze your rear delt and upper back muscles at the end position.
Return to the starting position with control and complete all reps before switching arms.
keywords: one arm cable rear delt fly
cable rear delt fly single arm
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Reverse Fly
exercises.overview: The cable single arm reverse fly targets the posterior deltoid and upper back muscles, helping to improve posture and shoulder health. The unilateral nature allows you to address imbalances while the cable provides consistent tension throughout the range of motion.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe and beneficial for most people, especially those with forward head posture. Start with light resistance to ensure proper muscle activation.

commonMistakes: Performing the lunge and row as separate movements
Leaning forward excessively during the lunge
Using too much weight and compromising lunge form
exerciseTips: Coordinate the lunge and pull to finish both movements simultaneously
Keep your chest up and maintain an upright torso
Pull your elbow back close to your body during the row
Step back far enough to achieve a proper lunge depth
instructions: Set the cable pulley to chest height and attach a D-handle.
Hold the handle with your right hand and step back to create tension.
Step backward with your left leg into a reverse lunge position.
As you lunge down, pull the cable handle toward your chest.
Return to standing while releasing the handle back to the starting position.
Complete all reps on one side before switching arm and leg positions.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Reverse Lunge
exercises.overview: The cable single arm reverse lunge combines lower body strength training with upper body pulling while challenging coordination and stability. It's an efficient exercise for building functional movement patterns.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with light resistance to master the coordination pattern. Ensure proper lunge mobility before adding the pulling component. Avoid if you have knee or shoulder issues.

commonMistakes: Rotating the torso excessively during the pulling motion
Using too much arm and not enough back muscle activation
Allowing the shoulder to roll forward at the start position
exerciseTips: Focus on pulling with your back muscles rather than your arm
Keep your torso stable and avoid rotating during the pull
Squeeze your shoulder blade toward your spine at the end of each rep
Maintain a slight bend in your knees throughout the movement
instructions: Sit at a cable row station and attach a single handle to the low pulley.
Position yourself with your working arm extended and grasping the handle, while your other hand holds the knee or thigh for support.
Keep your torso upright with a slight forward lean and your chest up.
Pull the handle toward your lower ribs by driving your elbow back and squeezing your shoulder blade toward your spine.
Hold the contraction briefly, then slowly return the handle to the starting position with control.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Row
exercises.overview: The Cable Single Arm Row allows you to work each side of your back independently while providing constant tension throughout the range of motion. This unilateral approach helps identify and correct strength imbalances between sides.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe but requires good core stability to prevent excessive rotation. Those with lower back issues should focus on maintaining proper posture throughout the movement.

commonMistakes: Leaning away from the cable during the press
Pressing too far behind the head
Using the non-working arm for assistance
exerciseTips: Keep your core engaged to prevent leaning
Press in a straight line overhead
Maintain a slight forward angle of the press
Control the eccentric portion of the movement
instructions: Set the cable pulley to about shoulder height and attach a single handle.
Stand with your side to the cable machine, feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the handle with the arm closest to the machine at shoulder level.
Press the handle straight up and slightly forward until your arm is fully extended.
Lower the handle back to shoulder level with control.
Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other arm.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Shoulder Press
exercises.overview: The cable single arm shoulder press is a unilateral exercise that builds shoulder strength while challenging core stability. It allows for natural movement patterns and helps identify strength imbalances between sides.
exercises.safetyInfo: Those with shoulder impingement or rotator cuff injuries should avoid overhead pressing motions. Start with light weight to assess shoulder mobility and comfort.

commonMistakes: Allowing the torso to rotate toward the weighted side
Pressing before fully extending the legs from the squat
Squatting too shallow to generate proper leg drive
exerciseTips: Keep your core engaged to resist rotation from the unilateral load
Coordinate the squat and press into one fluid movement
Maintain an upright torso throughout the squat portion
Press the handle straight up, not forward or to the side
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the lowest position and attach a D-handle.
Hold the handle at shoulder height with your right hand, facing away from the machine.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and lower into a squat position.
Drive through your heels to stand up while simultaneously pressing the handle overhead.
Lower the handle back to shoulder height as you descend into the next squat.
Complete all reps on one arm before switching to the other side.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Squat To Press
exercises.name_pt: Agachamento com Desenvolvimento Unilateral na Polia
exercises.overview: The cable single arm squat to press combines lower body power with unilateral upper body pressing while challenging core stability. The single arm loading creates anti-rotation demands and addresses muscle imbalances.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with light resistance to master the coordination and anti-rotation demands. Ensure adequate shoulder mobility before attempting. Avoid if you have shoulder or lower back issues.

commonMistakes: Using only the arms to pull without engaging the legs
Allowing the torso to round forward
Stepping down too quickly and losing muscle engagement
exerciseTips: Pull the cable as you drive up with your legs
Keep your chest up and core engaged throughout
Step down with control to maintain muscle tension
Focus on the stepping leg doing the majority of the work
instructions: Set a cable at low height and grasp the handle with the arm opposite your stepping leg.
Stand facing the cable machine beside a box or bench.
Step onto the box with your full foot while creating tension in the cable.
Pull the cable handle toward your body as you drive up through your heel.
Step down with control while maintaining tension in the cable.
Complete all reps on one side before switching legs and arm positions.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Step Up
exercises.name_pt: Subida no Banco Unilateral na Polia
exercises.overview: This exercise combines a step up with cable resistance to target the legs while engaging the posterior chain. It challenges coordination and provides constant tension throughout the movement.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure the box is stable and the cable path is clear. Start with lighter resistance to master the coordination before progressing weight.

commonMistakes: Moving the upper arm during the exercise
Using momentum instead of controlled movement
Allowing the elbow to flare out to the side
exerciseTips: Keep your upper arm stationary throughout the movement
Focus on squeezing your tricep at the full extension
Control the weight on both the lifting and lowering phases
Maintain a stable core to prevent swaying
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the highest position and attach a single handle.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, facing away from the cable machine.
Grab the handle with one hand and step forward to create tension in the cable.
Position your working arm overhead with your elbow pointing forward and upper arm stationary.
Extend your forearm by straightening your elbow until your arm is fully extended.
Slowly return to the starting position with control, keeping your upper arm still.
exercises.name: Cable Single Arm Tricep Extension
exercises.name_pt: Extensão de Tríceps com Um Braço na Polia
exercises.overview: The cable single arm tricep extension isolates the triceps muscle using a cable machine. This unilateral exercise helps identify and correct strength imbalances between arms.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have shoulder impingement or elbow pain. Start with light weight to establish proper form and control.

commonMistakes: Rounding the back instead of hinging at the hips
Allowing the hips to rotate or open up during the movement
Bending the standing knee too much or locking it completely
exerciseTips: Keep your hips square and avoid rotating during the movement
Maintain a slight bend in your standing knee throughout
Focus on pushing your hips back rather than just bending forward
Keep your core engaged to maintain balance and stability
instructions: Set the cable pulley to the lowest position and attach a D-handle.
Stand facing the cable machine holding the handle with your right hand, balancing on your left leg.
Hinge at the hips while lifting your right leg straight behind you, lowering the handle toward the floor.
Keep your back straight and reach forward until you feel a stretch in your left hamstring.
Drive through your left heel to return to the starting position while lowering your right leg.
Complete all reps on one side before switching legs and handle position.
exercises.name: Cable Single Leg Deadlift
exercises.overview: The cable single leg deadlift is a unilateral posterior chain exercise that challenges balance while strengthening the hamstrings and glutes. The cable provides constant tension throughout the range of motion.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with light weight to master the balance and movement pattern. Avoid if you have lower back issues or hamstring strains. Use a wall or support for balance if needed initially.

commonMistakes: Using the lower back instead of glutes to lift
Allowing the working knee to cave inward
Not achieving full hip extension at the top
exerciseTips: Squeeze your glutes hard at the top of each rep
Keep your core engaged to maintain stability
Drive through your heel, not your toes
Maintain tension in the cable throughout the entire range
instructions: Set up a cable at low height and attach an ankle strap to one leg.
Position yourself facing away from the cable machine with your back against a bench.
Place your working leg foot flat on the ground with the cable creating tension.
Lift your non-working leg up with knee bent at 90 degrees.
Drive through your heel to thrust your hips up, squeezing your glutes at the top.
Lower with control and repeat for all reps before switching legs.
exercises.name: Cable Single Leg Hip Thrust
exercises.overview: This unilateral hip thrust variation uses cable resistance to target the glutes while challenging stability and balance. It's excellent for correcting strength imbalances and building single-leg strength.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have lower back pain or hip injuries. Ensure proper ankle strap attachment to prevent slipping during the movement.

commonMistakes: Performing the squat and row as separate movements instead of coordinating them
Allowing the knees to cave inward during the squat portion
Pulling with the arms before engaging the back muscles
exerciseTips: Keep your chest up and core engaged throughout both movements
Coordinate the squat and row so they finish simultaneously
Pull your elbows back rather than flaring them out wide
Maintain tension in the cable throughout the entire movement
instructions: Set the cable pulley to chest height and attach a straight bar or rope attachment.
Stand facing the cable machine holding the attachment with both hands, arms extended.
Lower into a squat position while keeping your arms extended forward.
As you stand up from the squat, simultaneously pull the attachment toward your chest.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the row.
Return to the starting position by extending your arms and lowering back into the squat.
exercises.name: Cable Squat To Row
exercises.overview: The cable squat to row is a functional compound exercise that combines lower body strength with upper body pulling. It improves coordination while efficiently working multiple muscle groups in one movement.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with lighter resistance to master the movement coordination. Ensure proper squat mobility before adding the rowing component. Avoid if you have knee or shoulder issues.

commonMistakes: Bouncing at the bottom instead of controlling the movement
Not achieving full range of motion at the top
Bending the knees which reduces calf activation
exerciseTips: Keep your legs straight throughout the entire movement
Focus on achieving maximum range of motion both up and down
Pause briefly at the top to maximize muscle contraction
Control the descent to emphasize the eccentric portion
instructions: Attach a straight bar to a low cable pulley and place a calf block or platform in front.
Step onto the platform with the balls of your feet and grasp the cable bar with both hands.
Allow your heels to drop below the platform level to stretch your calves.
Rise up on your toes as high as possible by contracting your calf muscles.
Hold the peak contraction for a moment while squeezing your calves.
Slowly lower your heels back to the starting stretched position.
exercises.name: Cable Standing Calf Raise
exercises.overview: The cable standing calf raise is an isolation exercise that targets the calf muscles using constant cable tension. This exercise provides excellent muscle activation and allows for precise resistance control throughout the movement.
exercises.safetyInfo: Generally safe for most individuals. Those with Achilles tendon issues should start with lighter weight and avoid excessive stretching at the bottom position.

commonMistakes: Leaning away from the cable during the movement
Using momentum to swing the leg up
Allowing the working leg to rotate inward
exerciseTips: Keep your torso upright throughout the movement
Focus on lifting the leg using your glutes
Control both the lifting and lowering phases
Avoid swinging or using momentum
instructions: Attach ankle strap to low cable and secure around outside ankle.
Stand perpendicular to cable machine, holding onto machine for support.
Start with working leg crossed slightly in front of standing leg.
Lift working leg out to the side as high as comfortable.
Hold briefly at top, then control leg back to starting position.
Complete all reps before switching legs.
exercises.name: Cable Standing Hip Abduction
exercises.name_pt: Abdução de Quadril em Pé na Polia
exercises.overview: Cable standing hip abduction isolates the gluteus medius and minimus muscles, which are crucial for hip stability and preventing knee injuries. It's excellent for addressing weak glutes and improving functional movement patterns.
exercises.safetyInfo: Generally safe for all fitness levels. Start with light resistance to ensure proper movement pattern and avoid straining the hip joint.

commonMistakes: Arching the back to lift the leg higher
Using momentum to swing the leg back
Leaning forward during the hip extension
exerciseTips: Keep your torso upright throughout the movement
Focus on squeezing your glutes at the top
Avoid arching your back excessively
Control both the extension and return phases
instructions: Attach ankle strap to low cable and secure around working ankle.
Face the cable machine and hold on for support.
Start with working leg slightly in front of body.
Extend hip by driving leg straight back behind you.
Squeeze glutes at top of movement, then control return.
Complete all reps before switching legs.
exercises.name: Cable Standing Hip Extension
exercises.name_pt: Extensão de Quadril em Pé na Polia
exercises.overview: Cable standing hip extension isolates the gluteus maximus to build posterior chain strength and improve hip extension power. It's excellent for activating the glutes and addressing weakness in hip extension patterns.
exercises.safetyInfo: Generally safe for all fitness levels. Start with light resistance and focus on proper form. Avoid excessive back extension to prevent lower back strain.

commonMistakes: Rounding the back during the movement
Using momentum instead of controlled movement
Pulling too high toward the neck instead of chest
exerciseTips: Keep your chest up and core engaged throughout
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top
Maintain a neutral spine position
Pull with your elbows, not just your hands
instructions: Attach a T-bar handle to a low cable pulley and select appropriate weight.
Stand facing the cable with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent.
Hinge at the hips and lean forward, grasping the T-bar handle with both hands.
Pull the handle toward your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Slowly return to the starting position with control.
exercises.name: Cable T-Bar Row
exercises.overview: The cable T-bar row is a compound pulling exercise that effectively targets the lats and middle back muscles. It provides constant tension throughout the range of motion and allows for various grip positions.
exercises.safetyInfo: Those with lower back issues should use lighter weight and focus on proper hip hinge form. Avoid if you have shoulder impingement or recent shoulder injuries.

commonMistakes: Standing too upright instead of leaning forward
Allowing elbows to flare out to the sides
Using too much weight and losing proper form
exerciseTips: Keep your elbows tucked close to your sides throughout
Lean forward to mimic the dip movement pattern
Focus on pressing down and slightly back
Maintain constant tension on the cable
instructions: Set the cable machine to the highest position and attach a straight bar or rope attachment.
Stand facing the machine and grasp the attachment with both hands, arms extended downward.
Step back slightly and lean forward at a 45-degree angle with your feet staggered for stability.
Press the attachment down and back by extending your arms while keeping your elbows close to your body.
Slowly return to the starting position with control, allowing your arms to rise back up.
Maintain the forward lean throughout the entire movement.
exercises.name: Cable Tricep Dip
exercises.overview: The Cable Tricep Dip is a compound exercise that mimics the dipping motion using a cable machine for resistance. It effectively targets the triceps while providing more control and adjustability than traditional bodyweight dips.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with lighter weight to master the movement pattern. Avoid if you have shoulder or elbow pain. Ensure proper machine setup and stable foot positioning.

commonMistakes: Allowing the elbows to flare out to the sides during the movement
Using the shoulders or back to assist instead of isolating the triceps
Standing too close to the machine, reducing the effective range of motion
exerciseTips: Keep your elbows stationary and close to your head throughout the movement
Maintain a stable stance with a slight forward lean for balance
Focus on squeezing your triceps at the full extension
Control the negative portion of the movement for maximum muscle activation
instructions: Set the cable at the lowest position and attach a rope or straight bar handle.
Face away from the cable machine and step forward to create tension, holding the handle overhead.
Keep your elbows close to your head and pointing forward with upper arms perpendicular to the floor.
Extend your forearms forward and up by contracting your triceps until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly return to the starting position, feeling a stretch in your triceps at the bottom.
exercises.name: Cable Tricep Extension Overhead
exercises.name_pt: Tríceps na Polia por Cima da Cabeça
exercises.overview: The cable tricep extension overhead specifically targets the long head of the triceps through shoulder extension combined with elbow extension. This exercise is excellent for building tricep mass and strength in the stretched position.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise places the shoulder in an overhead position which may not be suitable for those with shoulder impingement. Ensure adequate shoulder mobility before performing.

commonMistakes: Moving the upper arm or shoulder during the exercise
Using momentum to swing the weight back
Not achieving full elbow extension at the end of the movement
exerciseTips: Keep your upper arm completely still throughout the movement
Focus on moving only at the elbow joint
Squeeze your triceps hard at the fully extended position
Use a controlled tempo on both the extension and return phases
instructions: Set a cable pulley to middle height and attach a single handle.
Stand with feet staggered, holding the handle with your upper arm parallel to the floor.
Keep your upper arm stationary and elbow bent at 90 degrees as the starting position.
Extend your forearm back by straightening your elbow until your arm is fully extended.
Squeeze your triceps at the full extension position.
Return to the starting position with control and repeat for all reps before switching arms.
exercises.name: Cable Tricep Kickback
exercises.overview: The cable tricep kickback is an isolation exercise that targets all three heads of the triceps muscle. The cable provides constant tension throughout the range of motion for optimal muscle activation.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe for most individuals. The cable provides constant tension which can be easier on the elbow joint than free weight variations.

commonMistakes: Allowing the elbows to drift forward during the movement
Using too much weight and involving the shoulders to complete the rep
exerciseTips: Keep your elbows locked at your sides throughout the entire movement
Focus on separating the rope ends at the bottom for maximum tricep contraction
Maintain a slight forward lean to optimize the angle of pull
Control the weight on both the pushing and returning phases
instructions: Attach a rope handle to the high pulley of a cable machine and grasp each end with an overhand grip.
Stand with feet hip-width apart, elbows at your sides, and upper arms perpendicular to the floor.
Push the rope down by extending your elbows while keeping your upper arms stationary.
At the bottom of the movement, separate the rope ends by pulling them apart and outward.
Squeeze your triceps hard at the bottom position before slowly returning to the starting position.
Control the weight as it returns to the top and repeat for desired repetitions.
exercises.name: Cable Tricep Rope Pushdown
exercises.overview: The cable tricep rope pushdown is an isolation exercise that effectively targets all three heads of the triceps. The rope attachment allows for a natural hand position and greater range of motion at the bottom of the movement.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe for most people and is often recommended for those with elbow issues. Start with lighter weight to master the movement pattern.
variations: Cable Tricep Pushdown with Straight Bar

commonMistakes: Pulling the elbows higher than shoulder level
Using too narrow a grip which can cause shoulder impingement
Allowing the weight to swing away from the body
exerciseTips: Keep your elbows higher than your hands throughout the movement
Pull the weight straight up along your torso
Stop when your elbows reach shoulder height
Control the lowering phase to maximize muscle engagement
instructions: Set a cable pulley to the lowest position and attach a straight bar or rope.
Stand facing the cable machine holding the attachment with an overhand grip.
Keep your hands closer than shoulder-width apart and arms extended.
Pull the weight straight up by raising your elbows until they reach shoulder height.
Keep the weight close to your body throughout the movement.
Lower the weight back to the starting position under control.
exercises.name: Cable Upright Row
exercises.overview: The cable upright row is a vertical pulling exercise that primarily targets the lateral deltoids and upper traps. The cable provides constant tension throughout the range of motion for optimal muscle development.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have shoulder impingement issues. Use a wider grip and avoid pulling elbows above shoulder height to reduce risk of shoulder injury.

commonMistakes: Using arms to pull instead of rotating through the core
Moving too fast and losing control of the movement
Keeping feet completely planted and restricting hip rotation
exerciseTips: Keep your core engaged throughout the entire movement
Allow your feet to pivot naturally as you rotate
Focus on the rotation coming from your core, not your arms
Control the weight on both the pulling and returning phases
instructions: Set cable at highest position and attach D-handle or rope attachment.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, side facing the cable machine.
Grasp handle with both hands, arms extended, reaching up and across your body.
Rotate your torso diagonally downward across your body toward the opposite hip.
Keep your arms relatively straight throughout the movement.
Return to starting position with control and repeat for all reps before switching sides.
exercises.name: Cable Wood Chop High To Low
exercises.name_pt: Wood Chop na Polia de Cima para Baixo
exercises.overview: The cable wood chop high to low mimics chopping wood motion, training rotational core strength and stability. This functional movement improves athletic performance and daily movement patterns involving rotation.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have lower back issues or recent abdominal surgery. Start with lighter weight to master the rotational movement pattern before progressing.
variations: Cable wood chop low to high

commonMistakes: Moving the forearms instead of isolating wrist movement
Using too much weight and compensating with momentum
Not achieving full range of motion at the bottom
exerciseTips: Keep forearms firmly pressed against your thighs throughout the movement
Control the weight on the way down for maximum muscle engagement
Focus on squeezing at the top of each rep
Use a full range of motion from full extension to full flexion
instructions: Start out by placing a flat bench in front of a low pulley cable that has a straight bar attachment.

Use your arms to grab the cable bar with a narrow to shoulder width supinated grip (palms up) and bring them up so that your forearms are resting against the top of your thighs. Your wrists should be hanging just beyond your knees.
Start out by curling your wrist upwards and exhaling. Keep the contraction for a second.
Slowly lower your wrists back down to the starting position while inhaling.
Your forearms should be stationary as your wrist is the only movement needed to perform this exercise.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Cable Wrist Curl
exercises.overview: Cable wrist curls target the wrist flexor muscles in isolation using constant cable tension. This exercise builds forearm strength and grip endurance essential for pulling movements.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with light weight as forearm muscles fatigue quickly. Avoid this exercise if you have carpal tunnel syndrome or wrist injuries.
variations: Seated One-Arm Dumbbell Palms-Down Wrist Curl

commonMistakes: Using too much weight and compromising form
Arching the back excessively during the raise
Moving too quickly and using momentum
exerciseTips: Lead with your thumbs pointing up toward the ceiling
Keep your core engaged to prevent arching your back
Focus on smooth, controlled movement throughout
Squeeze your shoulder blades slightly at the top
instructions: Set the cable pulleys to the lowest position and attach handles to both sides.
Stand between the cables and grab the left handle with your right hand and right handle with your left hand.
Step back to create tension with your arms crossed in front of your thighs.
Raise both arms up and out to form a 'Y' shape above your head.
Focus on lifting with your shoulders while keeping a slight bend in your elbows.
Lower your arms back to the starting position with control.
exercises.name: Cable Y-Raise
exercises.overview: The cable Y-raise targets the anterior and lateral deltoids while promoting shoulder stability and health. This exercise helps strengthen the shoulders in an overhead position commonly used in sports and daily activities.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with very light weight as this exercise targets smaller shoulder muscles. Those with shoulder impingement should avoid or modify the range of motion.

commonMistakes: Pressing through toes instead of balls of feet
Not achieving full stretch at the bottom position
Bouncing out of the bottom position
exerciseTips: Press through the balls of your feet, not your toes
Achieve full range of motion from stretch to peak contraction
Keep knees slightly bent throughout the movement
Control the negative portion slowly
instructions: Adjust the seat so that your legs are only slightly bent in the start position. The balls of your feet should be firmly on the platform.
Select an appropriate weight, and grasp the handles. This will be your starting position.

Straighten the legs by extending the knees, just barely lifting the weight from the stack. Your ankle should be fully flexed, toes pointing up. Execute the movement by pressing downward through the balls of your feet as far as possible.
After a brief pause, reverse the motion and repeat.
exercises.name: Calf Press
exercises.overview: The calf press machine allows for heavy loading of the calf muscles in a seated position. It effectively targets both the gastrocnemius and soleus for complete calf development.
exercises.safetyInfo: Maintain knee position throughout - avoid locking knees completely. Those with Achilles tendon issues should use reduced range of motion.
variations: Smith Machine Calf Raise Single Leg

commonMistakes: Allowing knees to bend during the movement
Placing feet too high on the platform
Not achieving full stretch at the bottom
exerciseTips: Position only the balls of feet on the lower portion of the platform
Keep legs fully extended but not locked throughout
Push through the balls of your feet, not the toes
Control the stretch at the bottom for maximum benefit
instructions: Using a leg press machine, sit down on the machine and place your legs on the platform directly in front of you at a medium (shoulder width) foot stance.

Lower the safety bars holding the weighted platform in place and press the platform all the way up until your legs are fully extended in front of you without locking your knees. (Note: In some leg press units you can leave the safety bars on for increased safety. If your leg press unit allows for this, then this is the preferred method of performing the exercise.) Your torso and the legs should make perfect 90-degree angle. Now carefully place your toes and balls of your feet on the lower portion of the platform with the heels extending off. Toes should be facing forward, outwards or inwards as described at the beginning of the chapter. This will be your starting position.

Press on the platform by raising your heels as you breathe out by extending your ankles as high as possible and flexing your calf. Ensure that the knee is kept stationary at all times. There should be no bending at any time. Hold the contracted position by a second before you start to go back down.
Go back slowly to the starting position as you breathe in by lowering your heels as you bend the ankles until calves are stretched.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Calf Press On The Leg Press Machine
exercises.name_pt: Panturrilha na Máquina de Leg Press
exercises.overview: Using the leg press machine for calf training allows heavy loading while maintaining stability. This variation effectively targets both heads of the calf muscles.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure safety bars are properly set before starting. Keep knees stationary - any knee movement shifts focus away from calves and increases injury risk.
variations: Smith Machine Calf Raise Single Leg

commonMistakes: Using too heavy a dumbbell and losing balance
Not controlling the rolling motion properly
Rushing through the movement without proper stretch
exerciseTips: Hold onto something sturdy for balance initially
Roll forward slightly at the bottom for maximum stretch
Control the rolling motion throughout the movement
Focus on the working calf while maintaining balance
instructions: Hang on to a sturdy object for balance and stand on a dumbbell handle, preferably one with round plates so that it rolls as in this manner you have to work harder to stabilize yourself; thus increasing the effectiveness of the exercise.
Now roll your foot slightly forward so that you can get a nice stretch of the calf. This will be your starting position.

Lift the calf as you roll your foot over the top of the handle so that you get a full extension. Exhale during the execution of this movement. Contract the calf hard at the top and hold for a second. Tip: As you come up, roll the dumbbell slightly backward.
Now inhale as you roll the dumbbell slightly forward as you come down to get a better stretch.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Calf Raise On A Dumbbell
exercises.overview: This advanced calf exercise adds an instability component by standing on a dumbbell handle. The rolling motion increases muscle activation and challenges proprioception.
exercises.safetyInfo: Always have a sturdy object nearby for balance support. Start with lighter dumbbells until balance and coordination improve. Not suitable for those with ankle instability.
variations: Smith Machine Calf Raise Single Leg

commonMistakes: Allowing arms to move and reduce band tension
Unequal band length causing imbalanced resistance
Not achieving full calf contraction at the top
exerciseTips: Maintain equal tension on both sides of the band
Keep arms stable in shoulder press position throughout
Rise up on toes as high as possible for peak contraction
Control the descent to maintain constant tension
instructions: Grab an exercise band and stand on it with your toes making sure that the length of the band between the foot and the arms is the same for both sides.

While holding the handles of the band, raise the arms to the side of your head as if you were getting ready to perform a shoulder press. The palms should be facing forward with the elbows bent and to the sides. This movement will create tension on the band. This will be your starting position.
Keeping the hands by your shoulder, stand up on your toes as you exhale and contract the calves hard at the top of the movement.
After a one second contraction, slowly go back down to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Calf Raises - With Bands
exercises.overview: Band calf raises provide variable resistance that increases through the range of motion. This portable exercise effectively targets calf muscles anywhere without weights.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure band is securely under both feet to prevent snapping. Check band for wear before use as sudden release can cause loss of balance.

commonMistakes: Rolling the shoulders backward during the shrug
Using momentum instead of controlled movement
Not achieving full range of motion at the top
exerciseTips: Squeeze your shoulder blades up toward your ears
Hold the peak contraction for one full second
Control the descent slowly
Keep your arms straight throughout the movement
instructions: Position yourself on the calf machine so that the shoulder pads are above your shoulders. Your torso should be straight with the arms extended normally by your side. This will be your starting position.
Raise your shoulders up towards your ears as you exhale and hold the contraction for a full second.
Slowly return to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Calf-Machine Shoulder Shrug
exercises.overview: This exercise uses a calf machine to perform shoulder shrugs, targeting the upper trapezius muscles. It provides a stable platform for building trap strength and size.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid rolling shoulders in circles as this can cause impingement. Keep the movement strictly vertical to prevent shoulder joint stress.

commonMistakes: Using too much weight before mastering the stability demands
Moving too quickly and losing control of the oscillating load
exerciseTips: Start with significantly less weight than your regular squat
Move slowly and deliberately to control the oscillation
Keep your core extremely tight throughout the movement
Focus on smooth acceleration out of the bottom position
instructions: Load the cambered bar and position it on your upper traps as you would a regular barbell.
The curved design will create an unstable load that moves during the lift.
Unrack carefully and stabilize the bar before stepping back.
Perform your squat while actively stabilizing the oscillating weight.
Control the bar throughout the entire range of motion.
Re-rack with extra caution due to the bar's instability.
exercises.name: Cambered Bar Squat
exercises.overview: The cambered bar squat uses a specially designed barbell with hanging weights that oscillate during the movement. It dramatically increases the stability demands and teaches superior motor control.
exercises.safetyInfo: The cambered bar creates significant instability and requires advanced lifting experience. Use spotters and start with very light weights to learn the movement pattern safely.

commonMistakes: Bending the arms during rotation
Not rotating through full range of motion
Using too heavy a weight and compromising form
exerciseTips: Maintain straight arms throughout the exercise
Rotate as far as possible in each direction
Keep your core engaged for stability
Control the movement speed
instructions: While standing upright, hold a barbell plate in both hands at the 3 and 9 o'clock positions. Your palms should be facing each other and your arms should be extended straight out in front of you. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the movement by rotating the plate as far to one side as possible. Use the same type of movement you would use to turn a steering wheel to one side.
Reverse the motion, turning it all the way to the opposite side.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Car Drivers
exercises.overview: An isometric shoulder exercise that involves rotating a weight plate like a steering wheel. This builds shoulder stability and endurance while challenging grip strength.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with lighter weight to master the movement pattern. Avoid jerky movements that could strain the shoulder joints.

commonMistakes: Using too much chain weight before mastering regular squats
Allowing form to break down during the variable resistance phases
Not adjusting chain length properly for your squat depth
exerciseTips: Focus on explosive concentric movement to overcome increasing resistance
Maintain proper squat mechanics despite variable loading
Control the descent even as the load decreases
Keep your core braced throughout the variable resistance
instructions: Set up chains on both ends of the barbell so they drape over the bar and partially rest on the ground.
Position the barbell on your upper trapezius muscles and step back from the rack with feet shoulder-width apart.
Maintain proper squat form with chest up, shoulders back, and neutral spine.
Descend by pushing hips back and bending knees until hip crease is below knee level.
As you descend, more chain links will rest on the ground, decreasing the load.
Drive up explosively through your heels, with resistance increasing as chains lift off the ground.
Complete the movement by fully extending hips and knees at the top.
exercises.name: Chain Squat
