exercises.name_pt: Levantamento Terra Romeno Unilateral com Halter
exercises.overview: This unilateral RDL variation targets the hamstrings and glutes while challenging core stability and balance. The single-arm loading creates an anti-rotation challenge for the core muscles.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have lower back injuries or severe hamstring tightness. Start with lighter weight to master the hip hinge pattern.

commonMistakes: Rotating the torso excessively during the pulling motion
Pulling the elbow out to the side instead of straight back
Using momentum instead of controlled muscle contraction
exerciseTips: Pull your elbow back rather than out to the side to maximize lat engagement
Squeeze your shoulder blade at the top of each rep
Keep your torso parallel to the floor throughout the movement
Focus on pulling with your back muscles, not just your arm
instructions: Place your left knee and left hand on a bench for support, with your right foot planted on the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in your right hand with your arm fully extended toward the floor.
Keep your back straight and core engaged throughout the movement.
Pull the dumbbell up toward your hip by driving your elbow back and squeezing your shoulder blade.
Pause briefly at the top with your elbow past your torso.
Lower the dumbbell slowly back to the starting position and repeat for all reps before switching sides.
exercises.name: Single Arm Dumbbell Row
exercises.name_pt: Remada com Um Braço com Halter
exercises.overview: The single arm dumbbell row is a unilateral pulling exercise that effectively targets the latissimus dorsi and supporting back muscles. It allows for a full range of motion and helps correct muscle imbalances between sides.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure proper bench setup to avoid losing balance. Those with lower back issues should pay extra attention to maintaining a neutral spine throughout the movement.

commonMistakes: Trying to muscle the weight up with arm strength only
Allowing the dumbbell to swing away from the body
Not dropping low enough to catch the weight properly
exerciseTips: Keep the dumbbell close to your body throughout the pull
Use your legs and hips to generate power, not just your arm
Catch the weight with a strong, stable overhead position
Practice the movement with lighter weight before progressing
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart with dumbbell on the floor between your feet.
Squat down and grasp the dumbbell with one hand using an overhand grip.
Explosively extend your hips, knees, and ankles while pulling the dumbbell upward.
As the dumbbell reaches chest height, pull yourself under it and catch it overhead.
Stand up to full extension with the dumbbell locked out overhead.
Lower the dumbbell back to the floor with control and repeat.
exercises.name: Single Arm Dumbbell Snatch
exercises.overview: The single arm dumbbell snatch is an explosive full-body movement that develops power, coordination, and unilateral strength. This exercise trains the entire kinetic chain from ground to overhead in one fluid motion.
exercises.safetyInfo: This is an advanced movement requiring good shoulder mobility and stability. Those with shoulder impingement or overhead restrictions should avoid this exercise. Master the movement pattern with light weight first.

commonMistakes: Leaning away from the weighted side during the lift
Allowing the shoulders to tilt or become uneven
Using knee flexion instead of proper hip hinge mechanics
exerciseTips: Keep your shoulders square and resist lateral bending
Engage your core to prevent compensation patterns
Focus on hip hinge movement rather than knee dominant squatting
Keep the kettlebell close to your body throughout the lift
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart with kettlebell positioned beside one foot.
Hinge at your hips and bend your knees to reach down and grasp the kettlebell.
Keep your chest up and maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement.
Drive through your heels and extend your hips to lift the kettlebell.
Stand tall with shoulders level and avoid leaning to one side.
Lower the kettlebell back to the floor with control and repeat.
exercises.name: Single Arm Kettlebell Deadlift
exercises.name_pt: Levantamento Terra com Kettlebell - Um Braço
exercises.overview: The single arm kettlebell deadlift trains unilateral hip hinge patterns while challenging core stability and anti-lateral flexion strength. This functional movement helps correct imbalances and builds real-world strength patterns.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise challenges core stability significantly due to the unilateral loading. Those with lower back issues should start with very light weight and focus on maintaining neutral spine alignment.

commonMistakes: Pressing the kettlebell forward instead of straight up
Allowing the wrist to bend backward under the weight
Leaning away from the weight instead of staying upright
exerciseTips: Keep your core tight to prevent excessive back arch
Press the kettlebell in a straight line overhead
Maintain a strong grip and straight wrist throughout
Keep your shoulder blade pulled down and back during the press
instructions: Clean a kettlebell to the rack position at your right shoulder with your palm facing forward.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and engage your core muscles.
Press the kettlebell straight up overhead until your arm is fully extended.
Keep your wrist straight and the kettlebell balanced over your shoulder joint.
Lower the kettlebell back to the rack position with control.
Complete all reps on one arm before switching to the other side.
exercises.name: Single Arm Kettlebell Overhead Press
exercises.name_pt: Desenvolvimento Unilateral com Kettlebell Acima da Cabeça
exercises.overview: The single arm kettlebell overhead press builds unilateral shoulder strength and stability while challenging core stability. The offset loading creates anti-lateral flexion demands on the core muscles.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure adequate shoulder mobility before attempting overhead pressing. Start with lighter weight to master the kettlebell rack position. Avoid if you have shoulder impingement or neck issues.

commonMistakes: Using the arms to lift the kettlebell instead of hip drive
Allowing the back to round during the hip hinge
Swinging the kettlebell too high above shoulder level
exerciseTips: Drive the movement with your hips, not your arms or shoulders
Keep your core engaged throughout to maintain stability
Maintain a proud chest position and neutral spine
Allow the kettlebell to float at the top rather than actively lifting it
instructions: Stand with feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Hinge at the hips and swing the kettlebell back between your legs while keeping your back straight.
Explosively drive your hips forward and squeeze your glutes to propel the kettlebell up to shoulder height.
Allow the kettlebell to swing back down between your legs under control.
Complete all repetitions on one side before switching to the other arm.
exercises.name: Single Arm Kettlebell Swing
exercises.name_pt: Swing com Um Braço com Kettlebell
exercises.overview: The Single Arm Kettlebell Swing is a unilateral power exercise that develops explosive hip extension while challenging core stability. It builds functional strength and power while improving coordination and balance.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have lower back problems or shoulder impingement. Start with lighter weight to master the hip hinge pattern before progressing to heavier loads.

commonMistakes: Lifting the elbow higher than shoulder level
Allowing the torso to lean away from the working side
Using momentum instead of controlled muscle contraction
exerciseTips: Lead with your elbow, not your hand
Keep the kettlebell close to your body throughout
Engage your core to prevent leaning to one side
Control the negative portion of the movement
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a kettlebell in one hand with an overhand grip.
Let the kettlebell hang at arm's length in front of your thigh.
Keep your core engaged and shoulders square to maintain balance.
Pull the kettlebell straight up along your body, leading with your elbow.
Lift until your elbow reaches shoulder height with the kettlebell at chest level.
Lower the weight with control back to the starting position.
exercises.name: Single Arm Kettlebell Upright Row
exercises.name_pt: Remada Alta com Um Braço com Kettlebell
exercises.overview: This unilateral variation of the upright row targets the shoulders and upper back while challenging core stability. The kettlebell provides a unique grip challenge compared to traditional barbells or dumbbells.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have shoulder impingement or rotator cuff injuries. Stop if you experience shoulder pain during the movement.

commonMistakes: Allowing torso to rotate during the press
Standing with feet too narrow
Pressing straight forward instead of following the arc
exerciseTips: Keep your core engaged to prevent rotation
Press in a natural arc following the barbell's path
Maintain split stance throughout the movement
Control both the pressing and lowering phases
instructions: Set up barbell in landmine attachment or corner, load one end with weight.
Stand in split stance with opposite foot forward from working arm.
Grasp end of barbell with one hand at chest level, elbow bent.
Press barbell forward and slightly upward in arc motion.
Control the weight back to starting position at chest.
Complete all reps before switching arms.
exercises.name: Single Arm Landmine Chest Press
exercises.overview: The single arm landmine chest press is a unilateral pressing movement that challenges the chest while requiring core stability. It's excellent for addressing imbalances and building functional upper body strength.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe for most individuals. Those with shoulder injuries should start with lighter weight and ensure proper range of motion before progressing.

commonMistakes: Allowing the torso to rotate excessively during the press
Pressing straight up instead of following the arc path
Using the legs to bounce the weight up
exerciseTips: Keep your core engaged throughout the movement to prevent rotation
Press in an arc motion following the natural path of the landmine
Maintain a slight forward lean of your torso during the press
Keep your wrist straight and strong throughout the movement
instructions: Set up a barbell in a landmine attachment or corner, loading weight on one end.
Stand facing the barbell and grab the loaded end with one hand at shoulder height.
Position your feet in a staggered stance with the opposite leg forward for stability.
Press the barbell up and forward in an arc motion, fully extending your arm overhead.
Lower the weight under control back to the starting position at shoulder height.
exercises.name: Single Arm Landmine Press
exercises.overview: The single arm landmine press is a unilateral pressing movement that challenges shoulder strength while requiring core stability. The angled bar path is more joint-friendly than vertical pressing movements.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally joint-friendly due to the arc motion path. However, ensure proper core stability to prevent compensatory movements. Start with lighter weights to master the movement pattern.

commonMistakes: Allowing the torso to rotate excessively
Using the biceps instead of back muscles
Not maintaining proper hip hinge position
exerciseTips: Keep your core tight to prevent rotation
Pull with your elbow, not your hand
Maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement
Focus on squeezing your shoulder blade toward your spine
instructions: Set up a barbell in a landmine attachment or secure it in a corner.
Stand perpendicular to the bar and grab the end with one hand.
Hinge at the hips and lean forward with a straight back and engaged core.
Pull the barbell up toward your ribcage, leading with your elbow.
Squeeze your back muscles at the top of the movement.
Lower the bar with control and repeat before switching to the other arm.
exercises.name: Single Arm Landmine Row
exercises.overview: The single arm landmine row is a unilateral pulling exercise that targets the latissimus dorsi while challenging core stability. The landmine setup provides a natural arc of motion that's joint-friendly while allowing for heavy loading.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise requires good core stability and hip mobility. Those with lower back issues should start with lighter weights and ensure proper hip hinge mechanics. Avoid excessive torso rotation.

commonMistakes: Arching the back excessively to help press the weight up
Pressing the weight forward instead of straight overhead
Using the non-working arm to assist the movement
exerciseTips: Keep your core tight to prevent excessive back arching
Press the weight straight up rather than forward
Maintain a neutral wrist position throughout
Keep your non-working arm relaxed at your side for balance
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell at shoulder height in one hand.
Keep your core engaged and maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Press the dumbbell straight up overhead until your arm is fully extended.
Keep the weight directly over your shoulder at the top position.
Lower the dumbbell back to shoulder height under control.
Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other arm.
exercises.name: Single Arm Overhead Press
exercises.overview: The single arm overhead press is a unilateral shoulder exercise that targets the deltoids while challenging core stability. Training one arm at a time helps identify and correct strength imbalances.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise challenges core stability significantly. Those with shoulder impingement or lower back issues should use caution and consider seated variations.

commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing the weight up
Arching the back excessively
Raising the weight too high above shoulder level
exerciseTips: Grip the dumbbell head firmly with both hands
Keep your core tight throughout the movement
Control the descent to maximize muscle engagement
Maintain a slight bend in your knees
instructions: With a wide stance, hold a dumbell with both hands, grasping the head of the dumbbell instead of the handle. Your arms should be extended and hanging at the waist. This will be your starting position.
Raise the weight until it is above shoulder level, keeping your arms extended. Your torso and hips should remain stationary throughout the movement.
Return to the starting position and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Single Dumbbell Raise
exercises.overview: The single dumbbell raise is a shoulder exercise that targets the front and side deltoids using both hands on one weight. This variation allows for heavier loading while improving coordination.
exercises.safetyInfo: Use a secure grip to prevent dropping the weight. Avoid this exercise if you have shoulder impingement or recent shoulder injuries.

commonMistakes: Allowing the hips to rotate during the movement
Using the non-working leg to push off the ground
Rushing through the movement instead of controlling it
exerciseTips: Focus on balance and control rather than heavy weight
Keep your hips square and avoid rotating
Use your free leg as a counterbalance
Engage your core throughout the entire movement
instructions: Hold a barbell with both hands and stand on one leg with a slight bend in your knee.
Lift your non-working leg slightly behind you for balance.
Hinge at the hip by pushing your hips back and lowering the barbell toward the floor.
Keep your back straight and the barbell close to your standing leg.
Lower until you feel a stretch in your hamstring or reach your mobility limit.
Drive through your heel to return to the starting position, then repeat for all reps before switching legs.
exercises.name: Single Leg Barbell Deadlift
exercises.overview: The single leg barbell deadlift is an advanced unilateral exercise that challenges balance, stability, and hamstring strength. It helps identify and correct strength imbalances while improving functional movement patterns.
exercises.safetyInfo: This advanced exercise requires excellent balance, mobility, and unilateral strength. Master bodyweight single-leg deadlifts before adding the barbell load.

commonMistakes: Allowing the extended leg to touch the ground for assistance
Overarching the back instead of focusing on hip extension
Using the hands to help lift the weight instead of the glutes
exerciseTips: Keep your extended leg straight and engaged throughout
Focus on driving through your heel rather than your toes
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top of each repetition
Maintain a neutral spine and avoid overarching your back
instructions: Sit with your upper back against a bench and a barbell positioned over your hips.
Place one foot firmly on the ground and extend the other leg straight out.
Hold the barbell in place with your hands for stability.
Drive through your planted heel and thrust your hips up toward the ceiling.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top with hips fully extended.
Lower your hips back to the starting position and complete all reps before switching legs.
exercises.name: Single Leg Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.name_pt: Hip Thrust com Barra - Uma Perna
exercises.overview: The single leg barbell hip thrust is a unilateral glute exercise that targets the gluteus maximus while challenging stability and balance. Training one leg at a time helps identify and correct strength imbalances.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise requires significant balance and core stability. Start with bodyweight only before adding the barbell. Those with lower back issues should ensure proper form to avoid hyperextension.

commonMistakes: Bouncing at the bottom instead of using controlled movement
Not achieving full range of motion at the top or bottom
Bending the knee of the working leg during the movement
exerciseTips: Keep your standing leg straight throughout the entire movement
Focus on a slow, controlled descent to maximize muscle activation
Squeeze your calf muscle hard at the top of each repetition
Maintain balance by engaging your core and using light fingertip support if needed
instructions: Stand on one foot with the other foot lifted slightly off the ground behind you.
Rise up onto the ball of your standing foot by pushing through your toes.
Lift as high as possible, squeezing your calf muscle at the top.
Hold for a brief pause at the peak contraction.
Lower back down slowly with control until your heel nearly touches the ground and repeat.
exercises.name: Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.overview: The single leg calf raise is a unilateral exercise that targets the calf muscles while standing on one foot. It helps develop calf strength, improves balance, and addresses strength imbalances between legs.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise is generally safe for most individuals. Those with Achilles tendon issues should be cautious and may need to reduce range of motion. Ensure proper balance to prevent falls.

commonMistakes: Bouncing at the bottom instead of controlling the stretch
Not achieving full range of motion at the top
Using the non-working leg to assist the movement
exerciseTips: Focus on the full range of motion from stretch to peak contraction
Keep your working leg straight throughout the movement
Control the descent to maximize the stretch
Hold the top position for a brief pause to increase intensity
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in one hand and stand on the ball of the opposite foot on a raised surface.
Let your heel drop below the platform to stretch your calf muscle.
Use your free hand for balance against a wall or rack.
Rise up onto your toes as high as possible, contracting your calf muscle.
Hold the peak contraction briefly before lowering with control.
Complete all repetitions on one leg before switching to the other side.
exercises.name: Single Leg Dumbbell Calf Raise
exercises.overview: The single leg dumbbell calf raise isolates each calf individually, helping to correct imbalances and build unilateral lower leg strength. The single-leg variation increases stability demands and allows for focused muscle development.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure you have adequate balance support when performing this exercise. Those with ankle injuries or Achilles tendon issues should use caution and potentially avoid this exercise.

commonMistakes: Allowing the hips to rotate or open up during the movement
Using the lifted leg to touch the ground for balance
Rounding the back instead of hinging at the hip
exerciseTips: Keep your hips square and avoid rotating during the movement
Focus on balance and control rather than speed
Keep the lifted leg in line with your torso
Maintain a micro-bend in the standing knee throughout
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and stand on one leg with a slight knee bend.
Hinge at the hip by pushing your hips back while lifting the non-standing leg behind you.
Lower the dumbbells toward the ground while keeping your back straight and chest up.
Descend until you feel a stretch in the hamstring of your standing leg.
Drive your hip forward and squeeze your glute to return to the starting position.
exercises.name: Single Leg Dumbbell Deadlift
exercises.overview: The single leg dumbbell deadlift is a unilateral exercise that targets the hamstrings while challenging balance and stability. It helps identify and correct strength imbalances between legs while improving functional movement patterns.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise challenges balance significantly. Start near a wall or support if needed. Avoid if you have ankle instability or acute hamstring injuries. Progress gradually with weight.

commonMistakes: Allowing hips to rotate or drop to one side
Pushing through the toes instead of the heel
Not fully extending the hip at the top
exerciseTips: Drive through your heel to maximize glute activation
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement
Keep your hips level throughout the exercise
Pull your knee toward your chest to increase difficulty
instructions: Lay on the floor with your feet flat and knees bent.
Raise one leg off of the ground, pulling the knee to your chest. This will be your starting position.
Execute the movement by driving through the heel, extending your hip upward and raising your glutes off of the ground.
Extend as far as possible, pause and then return to the starting position.
exercises.name: Single Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.overview: The single leg glute bridge isolates and strengthens the glutes unilaterally while improving hip stability. This exercise addresses muscle imbalances and enhances posterior chain development.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with bodyweight only and progress gradually. Avoid if you have acute lower back pain or hip flexor tightness.

commonMistakes: Using the lower back instead of glutes to drive the movement
Allowing the extended leg to drop or bend during the exercise
Not achieving full hip extension at the top of the movement
exerciseTips: Squeeze your glutes hard at the top of each repetition
Keep your core engaged to prevent your back from arching excessively
Drive through your entire foot, not just your toes
Maintain alignment between your knee, hip, and shoulder at the top
instructions: Lie on your back with one foot planted firmly on the ground and the other leg extended straight out.
Drive through the heel of your planted foot to lift your hips up toward the ceiling.
Squeeze your glutes at the top while keeping your extended leg straight and in line with your body.
Hold for a brief pause at the top of the movement.
Lower your hips back down with control and repeat for all reps before switching legs.
exercises.name: Single Leg Hip Thrust
exercises.overview: The single leg hip thrust is a unilateral glute exercise performed lying on the ground with one leg extended. It effectively isolates and strengthens the glutes while challenging core stability and balance.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise places significant demand on one side of the body. Those with lower back issues should ensure proper glute activation and avoid excessive back extension. Start with double-leg versions if needed.

commonMistakes: Rounding the back during the hip hinge
Bending the standing knee too much
Allowing the lifted leg to drop toward the ground
exerciseTips: Keep your back straight throughout the movement
Focus on hinging at the hip, not bending the knee
Maintain balance by engaging your core
Control both the lowering and lifting phases
instructions: Hold kettlebell in opposite hand from standing leg.
Stand on one leg with slight bend in knee.
Hinge at hip, lowering kettlebell toward ground while lifting opposite leg behind.
Lower until torso is parallel to floor or comfortable range.
Drive through standing heel to return to upright position.
Complete all reps before switching legs.
exercises.name: Single Leg Kettlebell Deadlift
exercises.name_pt: Levantamento Terra com Kettlebell - Uma Perna
exercises.overview: The single leg kettlebell deadlift is a unilateral hip hinge movement that builds hamstring and glute strength while challenging balance and stability. It's excellent for correcting imbalances and improving single-leg function.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with bodyweight or light kettlebell to develop balance and coordination. Avoid if you have balance issues or acute lower back pain.

commonMistakes: Allowing the hips to rotate or open up during the movement
Bending too much at the knee instead of hinging at the hip
Not extending the non-working leg back far enough to maintain balance
exerciseTips: Keep your hips square and avoid rotating during the movement
Focus on pushing your hips back rather than just bending forward
Use your extended leg as an active counterbalance, not just dead weight
Maintain a micro-bend in your standing knee throughout the movement
instructions: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand, with a slight bend in your standing knee.
Begin the movement by pushing your hips back and hinging forward at the waist.
Allow the non-working leg to extend straight back behind you as a counterbalance.
Lower the dumbbell toward the ground while maintaining a straight line from your head to your extended heel.
Feel a stretch in your hamstring, then drive your hips forward to return to the upright starting position.
Complete all reps on one leg before switching to the other side.
exercises.name: Single Leg Romanian Deadlift
exercises.overview: The single leg Romanian deadlift is a unilateral hip hinge movement that develops hamstring and glute strength while challenging balance and stability. It's excellent for correcting imbalances and improving athletic performance.
exercises.safetyInfo: This exercise requires good balance and hip mobility. Start with bodyweight only or very light weights until you master the movement pattern and balance requirements.

commonMistakes: Using too much shoulder instead of chest
Allowing the torso to rotate during the movement
Bringing the arm too far behind the body plane
exerciseTips: Maintain a slight forward lean throughout the movement
Keep a slight bend in your elbow during the entire range
Focus on squeezing your chest at the midline
Control both the concentric and eccentric portions
instructions: Begin by moving the pulleys to the high position, select the resistance to be used, and take a handle in each hand.

Step forward in front of both pulleys with your arms extended in front of you, bringing your hands together. Your head and chest should be up as you lean forward, while your feet should be staggered. This will be your starting position.

Keeping your left arm in place, allow your right arm to extend out to the side, maintaining a slight bend at the elbow. The right arm should be perpendicular to the body at approximately shoulder level.
Return your arm back to the starting position by pulling your hand back to the midline of the body.
Hold for a second at the starting position and repeat the movement on the opposite side. Continue alternating back and forth for the prescribed number of repetitions.
exercises.name: Single-Arm Cable Crossover
exercises.overview: The single-arm cable crossover isolates the chest muscles unilaterally while challenging core stability. This exercise helps address muscle imbalances and improves unilateral strength.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with lighter weight to establish proper form. Avoid if you have shoulder impingement or recent chest muscle strains.

commonMistakes: Pressing the bar at an angle instead of straight up
Not engaging the legs for power generation
Leaning too far back during the press
exerciseTips: Drive through your legs explosively
Keep your core tight throughout the movement
Press the bar in a straight line overhead
Maintain a wide stable stance
instructions: Position a bar into a landmine or securely anchor it in a corner. Load the bar to an appropriate weight.
Raise the bar from the floor, taking it to your shoulders with one or both hands. Adopt a wide stance. This will be your starting position.
Perform the movement by extending the elbow, pressing the weight up. Move explosively, extending the hips and knees fully to produce maximal force.
Return to the starting position.
exercises.name: Single-Arm Linear Jammer
exercises.overview: The single-arm linear jammer is an explosive overhead pressing movement using a landmine setup that develops unilateral shoulder strength and power while engaging the entire kinetic chain.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure the landmine is securely anchored to prevent the bar from slipping. Start with lighter weight to master the explosive hip and knee drive before adding load.

commonMistakes: Rotating the torso during the movement
Not maintaining proper plank position
Rushing through the eccentric phase
exerciseTips: Keep your feet wider than normal for better stability
Maintain a straight line from head to heels
Control the descent slowly
Keep your free hand behind your back
instructions: Begin laying prone on the ground. Move yourself into a position supporting your weight on your toes and one arm. Your working arm should be placed directly under the shoulder, fully extended. Your legs should be extended, and for this movement you may need a wider base, placing your feet further apart than in a normal push-up.
Maintain good posture, and place your free hand behind your back. This will be your starting position.
Lower yourself by allowing the elbow to flex until you touch the ground.
Descend slowly, and reverse direction be extending the arm to return to the starting position.
exercises.name: Single-Arm Push-Up
exercises.overview: The single-arm push-up is an advanced unilateral pressing exercise that builds exceptional chest, shoulder, and tricep strength while challenging core stability.
exercises.safetyInfo: This is an advanced exercise requiring significant upper body strength. Progress gradually from regular push-ups and assisted variations to avoid shoulder or wrist injury.

commonMistakes: Relying too heavily on the band for support
Using momentum to bounce up
Not controlling the descent
exerciseTips: Use the band only for balance assistance
Step up with control and step down slowly
Keep your chest up and core engaged
Focus on the working leg doing all the work
instructions: Position a box in a rack. Secure a band or rope in place above the box.
Standing in front of it, step onto the box to a full standing position, letting your other leg remain unsupported. Hold onto the band for balance
. Continue stepping up and down on the same leg before switching to the opposite side.
exercises.name: Single-Leg High Box Squat
exercises.overview: This unilateral exercise builds single-leg strength and stability while using assistance for balance, serving as a progression toward unassisted pistol squats.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure the box is stable and the band is securely anchored. Start with a lower box height and progress gradually to avoid knee strain.

commonMistakes: Setting the pad too low on the ankle instead of the shin
Using too much weight and compensating with body movement
Not aligning the knee with the machine's pivot point
exerciseTips: Keep your back pressed firmly against the seat
Control the lowering phase for 2-3 seconds
Squeeze your quadriceps at the top position
Avoid swinging or using momentum
instructions: Seat yourself in the machine and adjust it so that you are positioned properly. The pad should be against the lower part of the shin but not in contact with the ankle. Adjust the seat so that the pivot point is in line with your knee. Select a weight appropriate for your abilities.
Maintaining good posture, fully extend one leg, pausing at the top of the motion.
Return to the starting position without letting the weight stop, keeping tension on the muscle.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
exercises.name: Single-Leg Leg Extension
exercises.overview: The single-leg leg extension isolates the quadriceps muscles using a machine, allowing for unilateral strength development and muscle imbalance correction.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have acute knee injuries or patellofemoral pain syndrome. Start with light weight to assess knee comfort and gradually progress.

commonMistakes: Pulling on the neck with hands
Using hip flexors instead of abdominals
Coming up too high and straining the lower back
exerciseTips: Pull your ribcage toward your pelvis rather than just lifting
Keep your lower back pressed to the floor
Exhale forcefully as you crunch up
Focus on quality over quantity
instructions: Lie down on the floor placing your feet either under something that will not move or by having a partner hold them. Your legs should be bent at the knees.
Place your hands behind your head and lock them together by clasping your fingers. This is the starting position.
Elevate your upper body so that it creates an imaginary V-shape with your thighs. Breathe out when performing this part of the exercise.
Once you feel the contraction for a second, lower your upper body back down to the starting position while inhaling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Sit-Up
exercises.overview: The sit-up is a classic abdominal exercise that targets the rectus abdominis through spinal flexion movement.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have lower back problems. Consider alternatives like planks or dead bugs if you experience neck or back discomfort.

commonMistakes: Allowing arms to drift forward
Taking too large steps causing instability
Leaning forward excessively
exerciseTips: Keep your core tight throughout the movement
Maintain straight arms directly overhead
Take controlled steps to avoid jerky movements
Keep your head in neutral position
instructions: Attach dual handles to a sled connected by a rope or chain. Load the sled to a light weight.
Face the sled, backing up until there is some tension in the line. Hold your hands directly above your head with your elbows extended. This will be your starting position.
Walk backwards, keeping your arms raised above your head. Avoid jerky movements.
exercises.name: Sled Overhead Backward Walk
exercises.name_pt: Caminhada de Costas com Trenó Acima da Cabeça
exercises.overview: The sled overhead backward walk challenges shoulder stability and core strength while improving coordination. It's excellent for building overhead carrying capacity and postural strength.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have shoulder impingement or lower back issues. Start with very light weight to assess shoulder mobility and stability.

commonMistakes: Allowing elbows to flare outward
Using momentum instead of controlled movement
Dropping the upper arms during extension
exerciseTips: Keep your upper arms stationary throughout the movement
Step forward to maintain tension after each rep
Control the eccentric portion of the movement
Maintain stable foot position
instructions: Attach dual handles to a sled using a chain or rope. Load the sled to an appropriate load.

Facing away from the sled, step away until there is tension in the line. Raise your hands above your head, keeping them together, palms facing each other. Your elbows should be pointed upward with the elbows flexed. This will be your starting position.
Extend through the elbow to straighten the arm. Ensure that your upper arm stays in position to isolate the triceps.
Upon full extension, step forward to take the slack out of the line. You may keep your feet staggered for more stability.
exercises.name: Sled Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.name_pt: Extensão de Tríceps com Trenó Acima da Cabeça
exercises.overview: This isolation exercise targets the triceps using sled resistance, providing constant tension throughout the range of motion. It's effective for building triceps strength and size.
exercises.safetyInfo: Use light weight initially to avoid shoulder or elbow strain. Ensure proper warm-up of shoulders and elbows before performing.

commonMistakes: Using too much weight causing elbow flexion
Not fully retracting the shoulder blades
Allowing shoulders to roll forward
exerciseTips: Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together
Keep your arms straight throughout the movement
Control the return to starting position
Step back to maintain tension between reps
instructions: Attach dual handles to a sled connected by a rope or chain. Load the sled to a light weight.

Face the sled, backing up until there is some tension in the line. Take both handles at arms length at about waist level. Bend the knees slightly and keep your chest and head up. This will be your starting position.
Without flexing the elbow, pull the handles upward and apart, performing a reverse fly with some external rotation. Your palms should be facing forward as you do this.
Return to the starting position, taking a couple steps back to take the slack out of the line.
exercises.name: Sled Reverse Flye
exercises.overview: This isolation exercise targets the often-neglected rear deltoids and upper back muscles using sled resistance. It's excellent for improving posture and shoulder health.
exercises.safetyInfo: Use light weight to protect shoulder joints. Avoid if you have posterior shoulder impingement. Maintain proper posture throughout.

commonMistakes: Using arms instead of back muscles
Not fully retracting shoulder blades
Leaning too far forward
exerciseTips: Pull your elbows back past your torso
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top
Keep your chest up and core engaged
Step back between reps to reset tension
instructions: Attach dual handles to a sled connected by a rope or chain. Load the sled to an appropriate weight. Face the sled, backing up until there is some tension in the line.
With a handle in each hand, bend the knees slightly, keep your head and chest up, and begin with your arms extended.
To initiate the movement, flex the elbow as you retract your shoulder blades, pulling the sled towards you.
Take a step or two back to get tension in the line and repeat.
exercises.name: Sled Row
exercises.overview: The sled row is a compound pulling exercise that strengthens the entire posterior chain while providing functional movement patterns. It's excellent for building back strength and improving posture.
exercises.safetyInfo: Start with moderate weight to ensure proper form. Maintain neutral spine throughout. Avoid if you have lower back issues.
variations: Bent Over Two-Dumbbell Row With Palms In

commonMistakes: Using momentum to swing the weight up
Allowing the bar to drift away from your body
Lifting the weight too high past shoulder level
exerciseTips: Keep your core braced throughout the movement
Focus on lifting with your shoulders, not your arms
Control the descent to maximize time under tension
Maintain a slight bend in your elbows throughout
instructions: Place an incline bench underneath the smith machine. Place the barbell at a height that you can reach when lying down and your arms are almost fully extended. Once the weight you need is selected, lie down on the incline bench and make sure your shoulders are aligned right under the barbell.
Using a shoulder width pronated (palms forward) grip, lift the bar from the rack and hold it straight over you with a slight bend at the elbows. This will be your starting position.
As you breathe out, lift the bar up until your arms are fully extended. Note: The contraction should be felt around the shoulders.
After a second pause, bring the bar back down to the starting position as you breathe in.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
When you are done, place the bar back in the rack.
exercises.name: Smith Incline Shoulder Raise
exercises.name_pt: Elevação de Ombro Inclinada na Máquina Smith
exercises.overview: An isolation exercise targeting the front and side deltoids using a Smith machine for stability. The incline position allows for a more natural shoulder movement pattern while providing consistent resistance.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have shoulder impingement or rotator cuff injuries. The fixed bar path may not accommodate individual shoulder mechanics.

commonMistakes: Rolling shoulders forward during the movement
Using the arms to assist the lift
Shrugging forward instead of straight up
exerciseTips: Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top
Keep your chest up and shoulders back
Focus on moving only your shoulder blades
Hold the peak contraction briefly
instructions: With the bar at thigh level, load an appropriate weight.

Stand with the bar behind you, taking a shoulder-width, pronated grip on the bar and unhook the weight. You should be standing up straight with your head and chest up and your arms extended. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the movement by shrugging your shoulders straight up. Do not flex the arms or wrist during the movement.
After a brief pause return the weight to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before engaging the hooks to rack the weight.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Behind the Back Shrug
exercises.name_pt: Encolhimento por Trás na Máquina Smith
exercises.overview: A trap-focused exercise performed behind the body to emphasize the upper and middle trapezius. The behind-back position allows for a different angle of muscle activation compared to traditional front shrugs.
exercises.safetyInfo: This position can be uncomfortable for those with shoulder mobility issues. Start with light weight to assess comfort and range of motion.

commonMistakes: Bouncing the bar off the chest
Pressing with an excessively wide grip
Allowing wrists to bend backward under load
exerciseTips: Lower the bar to your mid-chest level

Control the eccentric portion slowly
instructions: Place a flat bench underneath the smith machine. Now place the barbell at a height that you can reach when lying down and your arms are almost fully extended. Once the weight you need is selected, lie down on the flat bench. Using a pronated grip that is wider than shoulder width, unlock the bar from the rack and hold it straight over you with your arms locked. This will be your starting position.
As you breathe in, come down slowly until you feel the bar on your middle chest.

After a second pause, bring the bar back to the starting position as you breathe out and push the bar using your chest muscles. Lock your arms in the contracted position, hold for a second and then start coming down slowly again. Tip: It should take at least twice as long to go down than to come up.
Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.
When you are done, lock the bar back in the rack.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Bench Press
exercises.overview: A compound pressing movement for chest development using the Smith machine's guided bar path. Ideal for beginners learning bench press mechanics or for training to failure safely.
exercises.safetyInfo: The fixed bar path may not suit all body types and can place stress on shoulders and wrists. Ensure proper bar position on chest to avoid shoulder impingement.

commonMistakes: Rounding the back during the movement
Using momentum to swing the weight up
Allowing the bar to drift away from the body
exerciseTips: Maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement
Pull the bar to your lower chest or upper abdomen
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top
Keep your elbows close to your body
instructions: Set the barbell attached to the smith machine to a height that is about 2 inches below your knees.
Bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward, by bending at the waist, while keeping the back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep the head up.

Now grasp the barbell using an overhand (pronated) grip and unlock it from the smith machine rack. Then let it hang directly in front of you as your arms hang extended perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.

While keeping the torso stationary, lift the barbell as you breathe out, keeping the elbows close to the body and not doing any force with the forearm other than holding the weights. On the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a second.
Slowly lower the weight again to the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Bent Over Row
exercises.overview: A compound pulling exercise targeting the middle back and rhomboids using the Smith machine for stability. The fixed bar path helps maintain consistent form while building back strength and thickness.
exercises.safetyInfo: Maintain proper spinal alignment to prevent lower back injury. Those with lower back issues should consider seated or supported rowing variations instead.
variations: Bent Over Two-Dumbbell Row With Palms In

commonMistakes: Bouncing at the bottom of the movement
Not achieving full range of motion

exerciseTips: Rise up on the balls of your feet as high as possible
Control the descent to feel a stretch
Keep your knees straight throughout
Pause briefly at the top of each rep
instructions: Place a block or weight plate below the bar on the Smith machine. Set the bar to a position that best matches your height. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, step onto the plates with the balls of your feet and place the bar on the back of your shoulders.
Take the bar with both hands facing forward. Rotate the bar to unrack it. This will be your starting position.

Raise your heels as high as possible by pushing off of the balls of your feet, flexing your calf at the top of the contraction. Your knees should remain extended. Hold the contracted position for a second before you start to go back down.
Return slowly to the starting position as you breathe in while lowering your heels.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Calf Raise
exercises.overview: An isolation exercise for calf development using the Smith machine for stability and safety. The fixed bar path allows for heavy loading while maintaining balance throughout the movement.
exercises.safetyInfo: Use a stable platform or block to allow for full range of motion. Ensure the platform is secure to prevent slipping during the exercise.
variations: Smith Machine Calf Raise Single Leg

commonMistakes: Allowing the non-working leg to assist the movement
Not achieving full range of motion at the top

exerciseTips: Keep your working leg completely straight throughout the movement
Focus on maximum range of motion both up and down
Control the movement to avoid bouncing at the bottom

instructions: Set up the Smith machine bar at shoulder height and place a calf block or platform underneath.
Position the bar across your upper back and step onto the platform with one foot.
Keep your working leg straight and allow your non-working leg to hang freely or rest behind you.
Lower your heel below the platform level to stretch your calf muscle.
Rise up on your toes as high as possible by contracting your calf muscles.
Complete all repetitions on one leg before switching to the other side.

exercises.safetyInfo: The unilateral nature of this exercise may challenge balance. Those with ankle instability should progress gradually and consider using additional support if needed.

After a second pause, bring the bar back to the starting position as you breathe out and push the bar using your triceps muscles. Repeat the movement for the prescribed amount of repetitions.

exercises.overview: A compound pressing variation that emphasizes triceps development over chest. The Smith machine provides stability allowing for focused triceps training with heavy loads safely.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid excessively narrow grips that may cause wrist strain. The close grip places more stress on the elbow joints, so warm up thoroughly.

exerciseTips: Retract shoulder blades and maintain arch throughout movement

instructions: Position a decline bench in the rack so that the bar will be above your chest. Load an appropriate weight and take your place on the bench.
Rotate the bar to unhook it from the rack and fully extend your arms. Your back should be slightly arched and your shoulder blades retracted. This will be your starting position.
Begin the movement by flexing your arms, lowering the bar to your chest.
Pause briefly, and then extend your arms to push the weight back to the starting position.
After completing the desired number of repetitions, rotate the bar to rack the weight.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Decline Press
exercises.overview: The Smith Machine Decline Press targets the lower portion of the pectoralis major with guided bar path stability. This exercise helps build lower chest mass and strength while providing safety through the machine's built-in safety mechanisms.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure decline bench is properly secured and positioned. Use safety stops set just below chest level. Avoid this exercise if you have shoulder impingement or lower back issues.

exerciseTips: Initiate movement with explosive hip extension
Keep bar close to body throughout entire movement

Land in athletic position with knees bent
instructions: Position the bar at knee height and load it to an appropriate weight.

Take a pronated grip on the bar outside of shoulder width and unhook the bar from the machine. Your arms should be fully extended with your head and chest up. Your elbows should be pointed out with your shoulders back and down. Your hips should be back, loading the tension into the hamstrings. This will be your starting position.
Initate the movement by forcefully extending the hips and knees, accelerating into the bar. Ensure that you keep your arms straight during this part of the motion.
Upon full extension, rebend the hips and knees to lower your receiving position.
Allow the arms to flex at this point, rotating the elbows around the bar to receive it on your shoulders.
Extend through the hips and knees to come to a standing position with the bar racked on your shoulders to complete the movement.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Hang Power Clean

exercises.safetyInfo: Master proper hip hinge mechanics before attempting. Start with light weight to learn timing and coordination. Avoid if you have wrist, shoulder, or lower back injuries.

exerciseTips: Focus on posterior pelvic tilt to engage abs

You may need to use your hands to assist you. For added stability grasp the sides of the Smith Machine. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the movement by rotating your pelvis, flexing your spine to raise your hips off of the bench. Maintain a slight bend in the knees throughout the motion.
After a brief pause, return the hips to the bench.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

After a second pause, bring the bar back to the starting position as you breathe out and push the bar using your chest muscles. Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Position your body in a straight line from head to heels with arms fully extended.
Pull your chest up to the bar by driving your elbows back and squeezing your shoulder blades.
Lower yourself back to the starting position with control, maintaining body alignment.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Inverted Row
exercises.overview: The Smith machine inverted row is an excellent bodyweight pulling exercise that targets the same muscles as pull-ups but in a more accessible way. It's perfect for beginners building up to pull-ups or as a back exercise for any fitness level.
exercises.safetyInfo: This is a safe beginner exercise. Adjust bar height to match your strength level - higher bar makes it easier, lower bar makes it more challenging.

exerciseTips: Position feet shoulder-width apart on bar
Drive through heels to extend legs powerfully
Maintain neutral spine against bench throughout
Control descent to maintain tension on muscles
instructions: Position a Smith machine bar a couple feet off of the ground. Ensure that it is resting on the safeties. After loading the bar to an appropriate weight, lie underneath the bar. Place the middle of your feet on the bar, tucking your knees to your chest. This will be your starting position.
Begin the movement by driving through your feet to move the bar upward, extending the hips and knees. Do not lock out your knees.
At the top of the motion, pause briefly before returning to the starting position.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Leg Press
exercises.overview: The Smith Machine Leg Press provides a horizontal leg pressing movement that targets the quadriceps while reducing spinal loading. This exercise offers an alternative to traditional squats with enhanced safety features.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure safety stops are properly set. Keep core engaged to protect lower back. Avoid if you have knee pain or ankle mobility restrictions.

instructions: Set the Smith machine bar at shoulder height and step underneath, positioning the bar across your upper trapezius muscles.
Step one foot forward 2-3 feet and one foot back, maintaining hip-width stance side to side.
Unlock the bar and lower your body by bending both knees until your back knee nearly touches the ground.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Complete all reps on one leg before switching sides.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Lunge
exercises.overview: The Smith Machine Lunge is a unilateral leg exercise that targets the quadriceps while providing stability through the guided bar path. It's excellent for building single-leg strength and improving balance with reduced risk of losing control.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have knee injuries or ankle mobility limitations. The fixed bar path may not accommodate all body mechanics, so ensure proper form before adding weight.

commonMistakes: Lifting elbow above shoulder height causing impingement
Using too wide grip reducing deltoid activation
Leaning away from working arm during lift
exerciseTips: Keep elbow higher than wrist throughout movement
Pull bar straight up along body midline

instructions: With the bar at thigh level, load an appropriate weight.

Take a wide grip on the bar and unhook the weight, removing your off hand from the bar. Your arm should be extended as you stand up straight with your head and chest up. This will be your starting position.
Begin the movement by flexing the elbow, raising the upper arm with the elbow pointed out. Continue until your upper arm is parallel to the floor.
After a brief pause, return the weight to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions before engaging the hooks to rack the weight.
exercises.name: Smith Machine One-Arm Upright Row
exercises.name_pt: Remada Alta Unilateral na Máquina Smith
exercises.overview: The Smith Machine One-Arm Upright Row targets the lateral deltoids and upper traps unilaterally with guided stability. This exercise helps correct imbalances while building shoulder width and trap development.
exercises.safetyInfo: Stop when upper arm reaches parallel to floor to avoid shoulder impingement. Start with light weight to assess comfort. Avoid if you have existing shoulder problems.

commonMistakes: Pressing the bar straight up instead of slightly forward

exerciseTips: Drive through your heels and maintain core tension
Press the bar slightly forward to follow natural shoulder arc
Keep your wrists straight and directly under the bar

instructions: To begin, place a flat bench (or preferably one with back support) underneath a smith machine. Position the barbell at a height so that when seated on the flat bench, the arms must be almost fully extended to reach the barbell.

Once you have the correct height, sit slightly in behind the barbell so that there is an imaginary straight line from the tip of your nose to the barbell. Your feet should be stationary. Grab the barbell with the palms facing forward, unlock it and lift it up so that your arms are fully extended. This is the starting position.
Slowly begin to lower the barbell until it is level with your chin while inhaling.
Then lift the barbell back to the starting position using your shoulders while exhaling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Overhead Shoulder Press
exercises.name_pt: Desenvolvimento na Máquina Smith (Acima da Cabeça)
exercises.overview: The Smith machine overhead shoulder press is a seated compound movement that builds anterior and lateral deltoid strength while providing stability and safety through the guided bar path.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have shoulder impingement or rotator cuff injuries. The fixed bar path may not accommodate individual shoulder mechanics.

commonMistakes: Allowing the extended leg to drop or bend
Shifting weight onto toes instead of heel

exerciseTips: Keep your extended leg parallel to the ground throughout
Descend slowly and control the movement

instructions: To begin, first set the bar to a position that best matches your height. Step under it and position the bar across the back of your shoulders.
Take the bar with your hands facing forward, unlock it and lift it off the rack by extending your legs. 3

Move one foot forward about 12 inches in front of the bar. Extend the other leg out in front of you, holding it off the ground. Look forward at all times and maintain a neutral or slightly arched spine. This will be your starting position.
Maintaining good posture, lower yourself by flexing the knee and hip, going down as far as flexibility allows.
Pause briefly at the bottom and then return to the starting position by driving through the heel of your foot, extending the knee and hip.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Pistol Squat
exercises.overview: The Smith machine pistol squat is an advanced unilateral exercise that develops single-leg strength, balance, and mobility while the machine provides stability assistance.
exercises.safetyInfo: Requires good ankle mobility and balance. Start with partial range of motion if you lack flexibility. Avoid if you have knee or ankle injuries.

commonMistakes: Using momentum to lift the toes
Allowing heels to lift off the platform
Bending at the knees during the movement
exerciseTips: Focus on lifting your toes as high as possible
Keep your heels firmly planted on the platform
Control the descent slowly
Maintain straight legs throughout the movement
instructions: Adjust the barbell on the smith machine to fit your height and align a raised platform right under the bar.
Stand on the platform with the heels of your feet secured on top of it with the balls of your feet extending off it. Position your toes facing forward with a shoulder width stance.

Now, place your shoulders under the barbell while maintaining the foot positioning described and push the barbell up by extending your hips and knees until your torso is standing erect. The knees should be kept with a slight bend; never locked. This will be your starting position. Tip: The barbell on your back is only for balance purposes.

Raise the balls of your feet as you breathe out by extending your toes as high as possible and flexing your calf. Ensure that the knee is kept stationary at all times. There should be no bending at any time. Hold the contracted position for a second before you start to go back down.
Slowly go back down to the starting position as you breathe in by lowering the balls of your feet and toes.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Reverse Calf Raises
exercises.overview: The Smith machine reverse calf raise targets the tibialis anterior muscle on the front of your shins, improving ankle dorsiflexion strength and helping prevent shin splints.
exercises.safetyInfo: Generally safe exercise with low injury risk. Ensure platform is stable and non-slip to prevent falls.

commonMistakes: Allowing the hips to sag or pike during the movement

exerciseTips: Keep your body in a straight line throughout the movement
Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together at the top
Engage your core to maintain proper body position

instructions: Set the Smith machine bar at about waist height and lie underneath it on your back.
Grab the bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your body straight from head to heels, with feet planted firmly on the ground.
Pull your chest up to the bar by retracting your shoulder blades and pulling with your back muscles.
Lower yourself back to the starting position with control, maintaining body alignment.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Row
exercises.overview: The Smith machine row is an inverted bodyweight rowing exercise that targets the lats and upper back. It's excellent for beginners learning proper pulling mechanics.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure the Smith machine safety catches are properly set. Those with lower back issues should be cautious and consider alternative rowing exercises.

commonMistakes: Lowering the bar too far below shoulder level, straining the shoulders

exerciseTips: Keep your back pressed firmly against the bench throughout the movement
Follow the machine's natural bar path without fighting it
Maintain steady pressure with both hands to keep the bar balanced
Focus on controlling both the pressing and lowering phases
instructions: Set up a bench inside the Smith machine with the back support at about 85-90 degrees.
Position the bar at shoulder height and sit on the bench with your back against the pad.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart and unlock the bar.
Press the bar straight up until your arms are fully extended overhead, following the machine's guided path.
Lower the bar back to shoulder level with control, stopping when your elbows reach about 90 degrees.
Repeat the pressing motion for desired repetitions, then lock the bar back in place.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Shoulder Press
exercises.overview: The Smith machine shoulder press is a beginner-friendly compound exercise that targets the shoulder muscles with added safety and stability. The guided bar path allows focus on form and progressive overload while reducing the need for stabilization.
exercises.safetyInfo: The Smith machine provides safety and stability, making it ideal for beginners. However, the fixed bar path may not suit all body types, so ensure comfortable range of motion before adding significant weight.

Taking too short of a step, causing knee to track over toes
Pushing off the back foot instead of the front foot
exerciseTips: Keep your torso upright throughout the movement
Focus weight on your front heel, not your toes
Descend until your back knee nearly touches the floor
Drive through your front heel to maximize quad activation
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar to shoulder height and step under it, positioning the bar across your upper trapezius.
Step one foot forward 2-3 feet and keep the other foot stationary behind you, creating a staggered stance.
Unlock the bar and lower your body by bending both knees until your front thigh is parallel to the floor.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
Complete all reps on one leg before switching to the other side.
keywords: smith machine rear foot elevated split squat
exercises.name: Smith Machine Split Squat
exercises.overview: The Smith Machine Split Squat is a unilateral lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps while improving balance and addressing muscle imbalances. The guided bar path provides stability for those learning single-leg movements.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid this exercise if you have knee injuries or ankle mobility issues. The fixed bar path may not suit all body mechanics, so ensure proper foot positioning before beginning.

commonMistakes: Placing feet directly under the bar
Allowing knees to cave inward
Descending too fast without control
exerciseTips: Keep your feet slightly forward of the bar
Descend by pushing hips back first
Drive through your heels to stand up
Maintain neutral spine throughout the movement
instructions: To begin, first set the bar on the height that best matches your height. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, step under the bar and place the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck) across it.
Hold on to the bar using both arms at each side (palms facing forward), unlock it and lift it off the rack by first pushing with your legs and at the same time straightening your torso.

Position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out. Keep your head up at all times and also maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position. (Note: For the purposes of this discussion we will use the medium stance which targets overall development; however you can choose any of the three stances discussed in the foot stances section).

Begin to slowly lower the bar by bending the knees as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calves becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the floor). Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front. If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the bar as you exhale by pushing the floor with the heel of your foot as you straighten the legs again and go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Squat
exercises.overview: The Smith machine squat is a compound lower body exercise that builds quadriceps, glute, and hamstring strength while providing enhanced stability and safety through the guided bar path.
exercises.safetyInfo: Safer than free weight squats due to guided bar path and built-in safety stops. However, the fixed path may not suit all body mechanics and can create unnatural movement patterns.

commonMistakes: Rounding the back during descent
Bending knees too much
Lowering the bar too far without hamstring flexibility
exerciseTips: Push your hips back while keeping chest up
Feel the stretch in your hamstrings at the bottom
Keep the bar close to your legs throughout
Drive hips forward to return to standing
instructions: To begin, set the bar on the smith machine to a height that is around the middle of your thighs. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, grasp the bar using a pronated (palms forward) grip that is shoulder width apart. You may need some wrist wraps if using a significant amount of weight.

Lift the bar up by fully extending your arms while keeping your back straight. Stand with your torso straight and your legs spaced using a shoulder width or narrower stance. The knees should be slightly bent. This is your starting position.

Keeping the knees stationary, lower the barbell to over the top of your feet by bending at the waist while keeping your back straight. Keep moving forward as if you were going to pick something from the floor until you feel a stretch on the hamstrings. Exhale as you perform this movement

Start bringing your torso up straight again as soon as you feel the hamstrings stretch by extending your hips and waist until you are back at the starting position. Inhale as you perform this movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Stiff-Legged Deadlift
exercises.overview: The Smith machine stiff-legged deadlift is a hip-hinge movement that primarily targets the hamstrings and glutes while the guided bar provides stability and safety.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if you have lower back issues or poor hamstring flexibility. The fixed bar path may force improper mechanics for some individuals.

commonMistakes: Pulling the bar too high to chin level
Using grip that's too narrow
Shrugging shoulders up toward ears
exerciseTips: Lead with your elbows throughout the movement
Keep the bar close to your body
Stop when the bar reaches chest level
Control the descent slowly
instructions: To begin, set the bar on the smith machine to a height that is around the middle of your thighs. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, grasp the bar using a pronated (palms forward) grip that is shoulder width apart. You may need some wrist wraps if using a significant amount of weight.
Lift the barbell up and fully extend your arms with your back straight. There should be a slight bend at the elbows. This is the starting position.

Use your side shoulders to lift the bar as you exhale. The bar should be close to the body as you move it up. Continue to lift it until it nearly touches your chin. Tip: Your elbows should drive the motion. As you lift the bar, your elbows should always be higher than your forearms. Also, keep your torso stationary and pause for a second at the top of the movement.
Lower the bar back down slowly to the starting position. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Smith Machine Upright Row
exercises.overview: The Smith machine upright row is a vertical pulling exercise that develops the trapezius and deltoids while the guided bar path ensures consistent movement pattern.
exercises.safetyInfo: Can cause shoulder impingement if pulled too high or with narrow grip. Avoid if you have shoulder problems or use a wider grip to reduce risk.

commonMistakes: Allowing front knee to cave inward
Leaning forward excessively during descent
Using back leg to push off bench for assistance
exerciseTips: Keep front knee aligned over ankle throughout movement
Drive through heel of front foot to engage posterior chain
Maintain upright torso with shoulders back
Control descent to maximize time under tension
instructions: To begin, place a flat bench 2-3 feet behind the smith machine. Then, set the bar on the height that best matches your height. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, step under the bar and place the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck) across it.
Hold on to the bar using both arms at each side (palms facing forward), unlock it and lift it off the rack by first pushing with your legs and at the same time straightening your torso.
Position your legs by placing one foot slightly forward under the bar and extending your other leg back and place the top of your foot on the bench. This will be your starting position

Begin to slowly lower the bar by bending the knee as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calf becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the floor). Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knee should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front. If your knee is past that imaginary line (if it is past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the bar as you exhale by pushing the floor with the heel of your foot mainly as you straighten your leg again and go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Switch legs and repeat the movement.
exercises.name: Smith Single-Leg Split Squat
exercises.name_pt: Agachamento Búlgaro Unilateral na Máquina Smith
exercises.overview: A unilateral lower body exercise that targets the quadriceps while improving balance and addressing muscle imbalances between legs.
exercises.safetyInfo: Avoid if experiencing knee pain or instability. The smith machine's fixed bar path may not accommodate natural movement patterns for all individuals.

commonMistakes: Starting second pull too early before bar reaches mid-thigh
Pulling with arms instead of driving with legs and hips
Allowing bar to swing away from body
exerciseTips: Maintain snatch grip width throughout movement
Keep bar close to body during both pull phases
Explode through triple extension at top
Focus on speed and power rather than maximum weight
instructions: With a barbell on the floor close to the shins, take a wide snatch grip. Lower your hips with the weight focused on the heels, back straight, head facing forward, chest up, with your shoulders just in front of the bar. This will be your starting position.

Begin the first pull by driving through the heels, extending your knees. Your back angle should stay the same, and your arms should remain straight. Move the weight with control as you continue to above the knees.

Next comes the second pull, the main source of acceleration for the pull. As the bar approaches the mid-thigh position, begin extending through the hips. In a jumping motion, accelerate by extending the hips, knees, and ankles, using speed to move the bar upward.

There should be no need to actively pull through the arms to accelerate the weight; at the end of the second pull, the body should be fully extended, leaning slightly back. Full extension should be violent and abrupt, and ensure that you do not prolong the extension for longer than necessary.
exercises.name: Snatch Pull
exercises.overview: A partial snatch movement that develops explosive pulling power and reinforces proper snatch pulling mechanics without the complexity of receiving the bar overhead.
exercises.safetyInfo: Lower risk than full snatch but still requires proper form. Focus on explosive hip extension while maintaining spinal alignment throughout movement.

commonMistakes: Allowing elbows to flare out to the sides during extension
Moving the upper arms instead of isolating the triceps
Using jerky momentum instead of controlled explosive movement
exerciseTips: Keep your elbows stationary and pointed forward
Extend arms explosively while maintaining control
Squeeze triceps at the top of each repetition
Maintain tension in the band throughout the full range of motion
instructions: For this exercise anchor a band to the ground. We used an incline bench and anchored the band to the base, standing over the bench. Alternatively, this could be performed standing on the band.
To begin, pull the band behind your head, holding it with a pronated grip and your elbows up. This will be your starting position.
To perform the movement, extend through the elbow to to straighten your arms, ensuring that you keep your upper arm in place.
Pause, and then return to the starting position.
exercises.name: Speed Band Overhead Triceps
exercises.name_pt: Tríceps Acima da Cabeça com Faixa Elástica em Velocidade
exercises.overview: This explosive tricep exercise uses resistance bands to develop speed and power in the triceps muscles. The variable resistance of bands provides accommodating resistance throughout the movement.
exercises.safetyInfo: Ensure the band is securely anchored to prevent snapping back. Avoid overextending the elbows at the bottom position to prevent hyperextension injury.

commonMistakes: Using too heavy weight that prevents explosive movement
Allowing knees to cave inward during the explosive ascent
Sacrificing depth for speed instead of maintaining full range
exerciseTips: Focus on maximum acceleration from the bottom position
Descend with control but ascend explosively
Maintain perfect form despite the emphasis on speed
Use 50-70% of your 1RM to allow for explosive movement
instructions: This exercise is best performed inside a squat rack for safety purposes. To begin, first set the bar on a rack that best matches your height. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, step under the bar and place the back of your shoulders (slightly below the neck) across it.
Hold on to the bar using both arms at each side and lift it off the rack by first pushing with your legs and at the same time straightening your torso.

Step away from the rack and position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out. Keep your head up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight back. This will be your starting position. (Note: For the purposes of this discussion we will use the medium stance which targets overall development; however you can choose any of the three stances discussed in the foot stances section).

Begin to lower the bar by bending the knees as you maintain a straight posture with the head up. Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calves becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the floor). Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement. Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front. If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.

Begin to raise the bar as fast as possible without involving momentum as you exhale by pushing the floor with the heel of your foot mainly as you straighten the legs again and go back to the starting position. Note: You should perform these exercises as fast as possible but without breaking perfect form and without involving momentum.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
exercises.name: Speed Squats
exercises.overview: Speed squats develop explosive power and rate of force development in the squat pattern. This training method improves the ability to accelerate through sticking points and builds dynamic strength.
exercises.safetyInfo: This advanced technique should only be attempted after mastering basic squat mechanics. Use a squat rack with safety bars set appropriately. Proper warm-up is crucial for explosive movements.

commonMistakes: Using only arm movement instead of full torso rotation
Moving too quickly without controlling the weights
Allowing the dumbbells to drop below parallel to ground
exerciseTips: Rotate from your core, not just your arms
Keep your arms straight throughout the movement
Maintain wide stance for stability during rotation
Control the deceleration at each end range
